message.content: Can you analyze our Linear and figure out what lane you can execute without disrupting other agent? and yes, use Linear MCP, dir to our knowledge base C:\Users\Nadeem\Desktop\Obsidian\build-blog\build-vault\5. Idea Vault\Application\B2B\Active\Canvasm - Easy Workflow, read agents.md, then understand other agent work then give me option for your lane

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

## claude.ai Supabase
Here are guidelines for using Supabase tools effectively:

- Before making schema changes, use `list_tables` to understand the existing structure
- When debugging issues, start with `get_logs` and `get_advisors` before making changes
- Use `get_project_url` and `get_publishable_api_key` when helping users configure client-side integrations

If you have access to a local development environment with a filesystem and shell:
- Install the Supabase agent skill for critical development and security guidance: `npx skills add supabase/agent-skills` (https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/ai-skills.md)
- Use the Supabase CLI (`supabase`) for local development workflows such as starting a local stack, managing migrations, and running edge functions locally (https://supabase.com/docs/guides/local-development.md)
- Prefer local development and testing before applying changes to a remote project

If you are running in a web-only or remote environment without filesystem or shell access:
- Rely on the MCP tools directly for all Supabase interactions
- Use `apply_migration` carefully, as changes go directly to the remote project

attachment.content:
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Scope

* **OAuth 2.1 authorization** (MCP-native) backed by Clerk: adding the server in Claude ([claude.ai](<http://claude.ai>) connector or `claude mcp add`) triggers a sign-in/authorize flow; the server then acts on the user's account, RLS-scoped.
* **Personal API key fallback** (<issue id="f12d3f61-4140-4981-8719-e366dc5e02bd" href="https://linear.app/canvasm/issue/CVS-89/settings-personal… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)","priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/canvasm/issue/CVS-99/mcp-auth-oauth-connect-sign-in-personal-api-key-fallback","gitBranchName":"mnadeemramli/cvs-99-mcp-auth-oauth-connect-sign-in-personal-api-key-fallback","createdAt":"2026-07-03T22:35:22.531Z","updatedAt":"2026-07-04T10:21:58.440Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":"2026-07-04T10:21:11.540Z","canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"In Review","statusType":"started","labels":["Feature","area:mcp","type:feature","source:vault"],"createdBy":"Nadeem Ramli","createdById":"3f0b8049-7811-47da-93f8-5d93770d4348","assignee":"Nadeem Ramli","assigneeId":"3f0b8049-7811-47da-93f8-5d93770d4348","project":"Programmatic building — Metrimap MCP + API","projectId":"592e6402-7367-4fe9-9950-376e6153a80d","team":"Canvasm","teamId":"7f02b994-356e-4594-bd76-fe95f22e08b5"},{"id":"CVS-100","title":"MCP server scaffold (hosted, remote transport, tool registry)","description":"**The server itself.** A hosted remote MCP server exposing Metrimap tools.

## Scope

* Remote HTTP/SSE MCP server (`mcp.canvasm.app`), deployed (Vercel/edge or dedicated service).
* Tool registry + dispatch; per-request auth context (from the auth issue); structured errors; logging.
* Calls the API/service layer (issue #2) as the authenticated user.

content.content:
=====ORCHESTRATION=====
---
draft: false
title: "Parallel Agent Build — Prompts and Orchestration"
date: "2026-07-04"
tags:
  - canvasm
  - agents
  - claude-code
  - orchestration
  - qa
---

# Parallel Agent Build — Prompts and Orchestration

For running Claude Code agents against the Linear **CVS** projects. Cowork plans and writes Linear; Claude Code builds from Linear (see [[metrimap-workflow]]).

## Can we run 4 agents at once?

**Not 1:1 with the four projects.** Two files funnel almost all canvas work: `src/features/canvas/pages/CanvasPage.tsx` (~2,200 lines) and `src/features/canvas/stores/canvasStore.ts` (plus `useCanvasActions.ts`, `useCanvasHistoryStore.ts`). If several agents edit those at once on `main`, you get constant conflicts and broken intermediate states.

Draw lanes by **file ownership**, not by project:

| Lane | Scope (issues) | Parallel? | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | Linear features & integrations — CVS-24, 25, 26, 27, 28 | ✅ fully | config, Supabase edge fn, GitHub Action, spikes — no React canvas code |
| **B** | Home redesign — CVS-29 | ✅ fully | `HomePage`/projects feature — separate from the canvas |
| **C** | Evidence — CVS-35, 36, then 34 | ⚠️ mostly | evidence files; only light `CanvasPage` overlap via `EvidenceNode` |
| **D** | In-canvas UX + Canvas engine — CVS-30, 31→32, 33, 37–44 | ❌ serialize | all funnel through `CanvasPage.tsx` + `canvasStore.ts`; real deps (CVS-38→43, 31→32) |

**Verdict:** run **A, B, C in parallel**; **Lane D is ONE agent** working its issues in dependency order (not 8 agents). That's ~3–4 concurrent agents, safely.

Two guardrails make it safe:
- **Branch per issue, not direct-to-`main`.** AGENTS.md already anticipates a preview/PR flow — switch to it for this parallel push. Use the Linear-provided `gitBranchName`. On one machine, use `git worktree` per lane.
- **Merge one PR at a time, rebase the others** — especially Lane D's serial issues.

**Prerequisite — run CVS-45 first.** The bootstrap issue installs the agent operating protocol into `AGENTS.md` and prunes stale docs. Every agent below assumes it's done.

---

## Shared build contract (prepend to EVERY agent prompt)

```
You are a Claude Code agent building Metrimap (React 19 + TS + Vite; React Flow @xyflow/react v12; Dagre/ELK layout; shadcn/Tailwind; Zustand + XState; Supabase + Clerk; Vercel). AGENTS.md is the source of truth for architecture, commands, and conventions — read it first. The product Obsidian vault (…/Canvasm - Easy Workflow) holds product memory; read and update it when relevant.

Work items live in Linear (team CVS), organized as Initiatives → Projects → Issues. Start from your assigned Project and take the next issue by priority + unblocked status (respect blockedBy). For EACH issue:

1. Read the issue in full — description AND all comments. Many carry a "## Deep analysis" comment (current code with file:line refs) and a "## Decision (locked)" comment. Honor the locked decisions.
2. Create a branch from the issue's `gitBranchName` (Linear provides it). Do NOT commit straight to main during this parallel push.
3. Implement to satisfy the acceptance criteria + locked decisions. Match surrounding code; reuse src/shared/components/ui (shadcn) before adding deps. Schema changes: add a migration under supabase/migrations/, run `npm run prisma:types`, update src/shared/lib/supabase/types.ts.
4. Verify: `npm run type-check`, `npm run lint`, `npm run test` (and `npm run test:rls` if RLS is touched). Do NOT bypass the Husky pre-commit hook.
5. MANDATORY — create a manual-test SUB-ISSUE (child of the feature issue): title "Manual test: CVS-XX — <feature>", placed in the Manual Test project, labelled manual-test, assigned to the owner (m.nadeemramli@gmail.com — shows in their Todo). The body MUST contain:
   - a link to the parent issue + the PR,
   - a one-paragraph change summary + preconditions,
   - numbered test cases derived from EACH acceptance criterion (steps → expected result),
   - edge cases, and a pass/fail checkbox per case.
   Model the cases on the vault "Manual QA Finding Template". This sub-issue is the human's vetting task — no feature is "done" without a passing one. Do NOT write a manual-test doc file; tests are Linear sub-issues now. (No dedicated tester account — route by the Manual Test project.)
6. Open a PR that references the issue (keep "CVS-XX" in the branch/PR so Linear links it); link the manual-test sub-issue in the PR.
7. Move the feature issue to "In Review" (NOT Done). The feature is Done only after a human runs and passes the manual-test sub-issue (in the Manual Test project) and the change is merged/released.

Never expose secrets. Keep Supabase error/evidence stores and RLS intact. If you must edit a shared-hotspot file another lane may be in (CanvasPage.tsx, canvasStore.ts), keep the diff tight and rebase before opening the PR.
```

---

## Agent A — Linear features & integrations (Lane A · fully parallel)

```
[Prepend the Shared build contract.]

Your project: "Linear features & integrations". Issues, in order:
- CVS-24 — Initiatives are already created (the workspace now has them). Finish only the remaining part: map each Initiative's objective to a metric-tree node and post the first Initiative update. (Likely closeable quickly.)
- CVS-26 — Userback → Customer Requests bridge. Server-side edge function: Userback API/webhook → Linear customerCreate (map email domain) + customerNeedCreate. Keys stay server-side. Self-contained; no React canvas code.
- CVS-25 — Releases pipeline. ⚠️ Requires a Business+ Linear plan — CONFIRM the plan first; if not available, stop and report. Otherwise add the linear-release GitHub Action on push to main (repo secret LINEAR_ACCESS_KEY) + status automation.
- CVS-27 — Evaluate Sentry (spike). Deliverable is a written recommendation on the issue; only wire a prototype if cheap. Manual-test sub-issue only if you actually wire something.
- CVS-28 — Evaluate Snyk (spike). Enable GitHub PR checks on the repo; recommend which findings route to Linear. Recommendation on the issue.

This lane touches config / edge functions / CI only — safe to run alongside B, C, D.
```

## Agent B — Home redesign (Lane B · fully parallel)

```
[Prepend the Shared build contract.]

Your single issue: CVS-29 — "Redesign canvases home". Files: src/features/projects/pages/HomePage.tsx and related. Follow the direction in the issue and the planning mockup: one consolidated control bar (space · All/Recent/Starred · search · sort · view · a single "New" split button); collapse the Examples carousel to a slim dismissible entry that remembers its state; user canvases become the primary above-the-fold content. Consider running the design-handoff skill first for exact tokens/specs. Keep every current affordance reachable; WCAG AA; responsive. Separate from the canvas — safe in parallel.
```

## Agent C — Evidence (Lane C · mostly parallel; coordinate CanvasPage)

```
[Prepend the Shared build contract.]

Your project: "Evidence Notebook refactor". Order:
1. CVS-35 — canvas card "No content available". Root cause in EvidenceContentRenderer.tsx: it renders only when evidence.content (EditorJS) is set, else falls back to summary/"No content available". Render populated summary/hypothesis, and fix the field-mapping so the node gets the same data as the full view.
2. CVS-36 — "View Full" wiring. EvidenceNode.tsx uses <Link to=/evidence/:id>; fix the route/page OR switch to an in-canvas side panel (decide + implement consistently).
3. CVS-34 — Notebook-style refactor (umbrella). Unify node preview + full view + comments around the one EditorJS notebook. Folds in comments; reuse the CVS-33 codename utility (coordinate with Lane D — do not duplicate it).

Files: EvidenceContentRenderer.tsx, EvidenceNode.tsx, EvidencePage.tsx, editorjs-config.ts. Light CanvasPage overlap via EvidenceNode — rebase before merging.
```

## Agent D — In-canvas UX + Canvas engine (Lane D · SERIAL, one agent)

```
[Prepend the Shared build contract.]

You own CanvasPage.tsx + canvasStore.ts, so this lane is NOT split — work issues ONE AT A TIME, each on its own branch → PR → manual-test-doc → In Review, in this dependency order:

1. CVS-33 — comment codename utility (build this FIRST; it's shared — Lane C's CVS-34 reuses it).
2. CVS-30 — off-screen wayfinding indicator.
3. CVS-31 — consolidate canvas toolbar  →  4. CVS-32 — relocate toolbar top-right (blocked by 31).
5. CVS-38 — node intersections (foundation)  →  6. CVS-43 — grouping + subflows (blocked by 38; true nested subflows with on-canvas UI per the locked decision).
7. CVS-41 — copy/paste (extend the existing useCanvasActions clipboard; add edges-in-selection + cross-canvas).
8. CVS-42 — undo/redo (extend the existing useCanvasHistoryStore command-stack to cover moves/edits/edge ops; route through updateNodePosition/autosave per the locked decision).
9. CVS-40 — realtime collaboration (fix the LWW "one step behind" reconciliation via applyNodeChanges/setNodes; CRDT is a later phase — do NOT build it now).
10. CVS-37 — easy connect.   11. CVS-39 — node position animation.
12. CVS-44 — edge routing (libavoid, live obstacle-avoidance — heaviest, LAST).

Honor each issue's "Decision (locked)" comment. Undo/redo UI buttons + auto-layout/groups controls land in the CVS-31 toolbar.
```

---

## Why this makes a good feedback loop

Every completed issue lands as: a **branch + PR** (traceable), a **manual-test sub-issue in the Manual Test project** with runnable test cases, and the feature issue in **In Review** (not Done). Nothing closes until a human passes the sub-issue — so "complete" always means "vetted." This is the verification loop from [[3.a Workflow Architecture - Linear Obsidian Agents Product Surfaces CI-CD]] and [[2.a.iv Quality and Verification Pipeline]] made concrete.

## Related
- [[1. Development Operating System]] · [[metrimap-workflow]] · [[2.a.iv Quality and Verification Pipeline]] · [[Development Backlog by Intake Group]]

```
1. CVS-35 — canvas card "No content available". Root cause in EvidenceContentRenderer.tsx: it renders only when evidence.content (EditorJS) is set, else falls back to summary/"No content available". Render populated summary/hypothesis, and fix the field-mapping so the node gets the same data as the full view.
2. CVS-36 — "View Full" wiring. EvidenceNode.tsx uses <Link to=/evidence/:id>; fix the route/page OR switch to an in-canvas side panel (decide + implement consistently).
3. CVS-34 — Notebook-style refactor (umbrella). Unify node preview + full view + comments around the one EditorJS notebook. Folds in comments; reuse the CVS-33 codename utility (coordinate with Lane D — do not duplicate it).

Files: EvidenceContentRenderer.tsx, EvidenceNode.tsx, EvidencePage.tsx, editorjs-config.ts. Light CanvasPage overlap via EvidenceNode — rebase before merging.
```

## Agent D — In-canvas UX + Canvas engine (Lane D · SERIAL, one agent)

```
[Prepend the Shared build contract.]

You own CanvasPage.tsx + canvasStore.ts, so this lane is NOT split — work issues ONE AT A TIME, each on its own branch → PR → manual-test-doc → In Review, in this dependency order:

1. CVS-33 — comment codename utility (build this FIRST; it's shared — Lane C's CVS-34 reuses it).
2. CVS-30 — off-screen wayfinding indicator.
3. CVS-31 — consolidate canvas toolbar  →  4. CVS-32 — relocate toolbar top-right (blocked by 31).
5. CVS-38 — node intersections (foundation)  →  6. CVS-43 — grouping + subflows (blocked by 38; true nested subflows with on-canvas UI per the locked decision).
7. CVS-41 — copy/paste (extend the existing useCanvasActions clipboard; add edges-in-selection + cross-canvas).
8. CVS-42 — undo/redo (extend the existing useCanvasHistoryStore command-stack to cover moves/edits/edge ops; route through updateNodePosition/autosave per the locked decision).
9. CVS-40 — realtime collaboration (fix the LWW "one step behind" reconciliation via applyNodeChanges/setNodes; CRDT is a later phase — do NOT build it now).
10. CVS-37 — easy connect.   11. CVS-39 — node position animation.
12. CVS-44 — edge routing (libavoid, live obstacle-avoidance — heaviest, LAST).

Honor each issue's "Decision (locked)" comment. Undo/redo UI buttons + auto-layout/groups controls land in the CVS-31 toolbar.
```

---

## Why this makes a good feedback loop

Every completed issue lands as: a **branch + PR** (traceable), a **manual-test sub-issue in the Manual Test project** with runnable test cases, and the feature issue in **In Review** (not Done). Nothing closes until a human passes the sub-issue — so "complete" always means "vetted." This is the verification loop from [[3.a Workflow Architecture - Linear Obsidian Agents Product Surfaces CI-CD]] and [[2.a.iv Quality and Verification Pipeline]] made concrete.

## Related
- [[1. Development Operating System]] · [[metrimap-workflow]] · [[2.a.iv Quality and Verification Pipeline]] · [[Development Backlog by Intake Group]]

{"issues":[{"id":"CVS-37","title":"Easy Connect — start edges from anywhere on a node","description":"**Group 2 — Product Evolution (canvas nodes).** Let users start a connection by dragging from anywhere on a node (whole-node connect), not only from a specific handle — faster edge creation.

## Ref

* [https://reactflow.dev/examples/nodes/easy-connect](<https://reactflow.dev/examples/nodes/easy-connect>)

===== ACCESS & VISIBILITY PRD ===== --- draft: false title: "Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency)" date: "2026-07-04" tags: - canvasm - product - permissions - security - rls - design --- # Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency) — Design Design doc for **node-level access control** on the canvas. Shaped output of the design spike (Linear **CVS-118**) so the build issues **CVS-119 → CVS-123** can proceed. Project: *Access & visibility (node-level transparency)*. ## Why this matters The driver tree is meant to be the **shared source of truth that drives the whole team** — everyone reasons off the same map. But **not everything can be shown to everyone**: Net Profit, margins, payroll, CAC/LTV by channel, revenue by customer. Without granular visibility a business owner is stuck in a lose-lose: - **over-share** → leak sensitive numbers to people who shouldn't see them, or - **don't share** → the tree stops being a team tool and reverts to a private artifact. **Node-level visibility is what makes a company-wide driver tree safe to share.** Finance sees the whole picture; Marketing sees their branch; nobody sees Net Profit who shouldn't — on the *same* canvas. ## Current state (what we build on) - **Org-collaborative permissions** (PRD `5. Current State/3. Permissions and Roles`): workspace = Clerk org; **all members get full edit**. Roles (Viewer / Commenter / Editor / Admin) only gate **outside guests**. **No within-workspace tiering yet** — so today Finance and Marketing in the same workspace see everything. - **Tags already exist:** `tags`, `metric_card_tags`, `relationship_tags`; cards carry `tags[]`. - **RLS** is workspace-scoped and Clerk-hardened; `permission_tiers` + `metric_values` migrations exist; `npm run test:rls` covers the guest tiers. So this **extends** the model — it doesn't invent a new one. ## The model — tag × group, enforced at RLS ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Viewer (member of Marketing)"] -->|reads canvas| R["RLS on cards + metric_values"] R -->|viewer's groups ∩ node audience| D{"Allowed?"} D -- yes --> V["Show node + value"] D -- no (hide-value) --> M["Show node, value = 'Restricted'"] D -- no (hide-node) --> H["Node + its edges omitted"] N["Net Profit node
access tag: finance-only"] --> R ``` 1. **Groups / departments (new):** groups *within* a workspace (Finance, Marketing, Exec); a user belongs to 1+. Introduces internal tiering that doesn't exist today. 2. **Access tags with an audience:** reuse the existing tag system — a tag can be marked an **access tag** carrying an **audience** (allowed groups). Assign it when creating/editing a node (and on dashboards). **No access tag = visible to everyone with canvas access.** 3. **Two redaction modes** (per node / policy): - **Hide value** (default): node + relationships stay, the number/series shows **"Restricted"** — preserves the tree's shape so the map still reads. - **Hide node**: node + its edges are omitted for unauthorized viewers — for truly secret items. 4. **RLS is the hard boundary:** policies on `cards`/nodes and `metric_values` check the viewer's **group membership ∩ node access-tag audience**. Unauthorized rows are filtered (hide-node) or value-redacted (hide-value). **A marketer can't fetch Net Profit even via the API or MCP.** The UI is convenience; RLS is the wall. 5. **Dashboard / canvas audience:** assign an audience to a dashboard or shared view so it only surfaces permitted metrics; the existing share flow (People tab / roles) honors node visibility. ## The hard problem — information leakage A restricted node that **drives** a visible metric can leak through the **edge** (its existence/label) or the **derived value** (you can back it out). This is the real design call: - Hide **edges** touching restricted nodes from unauthorized viewers (baseline). - Decide whether metrics **derived from** restricted inputs are **also masked** — strict (mask anything downstream of a secret) vs. permissive (show the output, hide the input). Recommend **configurable**, default strict for anything one hop from a restricted input. ## Enhancement — access derived from the data source From the motivating example (an accounting connection only accountants can reach): a metric **sourced from a restricted connection** could **inherit** restriction automatically — access follows the data source. Cleaner than tagging each node by hand. (Ties to Connected accounts, CVS-90.) ## Decisions the spike settles - Group model: new `workspace_groups` + `group_members` vs. extend Clerk roles. (Recommend new groups; Clerk org stays the tenant boundary.) - Access-tag model: audience on a tag vs. per-node allowlist (support both; tag is the norm, allowlist the escape hatch). - Redaction default: **hide-value**. - Info-leak policy: edge hiding + derived-metric masking (strict default, configurable). - Data-source-derived access: in or out of v1. - Exact RLS predicates on `cards` + `metric_values`. ## Data-model additions - `workspace_groups`, `group_members` (RLS-scoped). - Access-tag audience: `tag_audiences` (tag → group) or an `is_access` + audience on tags. - Node redaction mode (per node/tag). - RLS policies on `cards`, edges/relationships, `metric_values`. - `visibility_audit` (who set what audience, when). ## UX - Per-node **"Who can see this"** control + a **lock / Restricted badge**. - Redacted rendering for unauthorized viewers (masked value or omitted node). - Admin **access matrix** (access tags × groups) + **"view as "** preview for owners. - Dashboard **audience picker** + "hides N restricted metrics for this audience". ## Phased plan (Linear) `CVS-118` spike (this) → **`CVS-119`** groups · **`CVS-120`** access tags + policy → **`CVS-121`** RLS enforcement → **`CVS-122`** redaction UX + admin matrix · **`CVS-123`** dashboard audience. Critical path: spike → (groups + tags) → RLS → (UX + dashboard). ## Principles 1. **RLS is the boundary; UI is convenience.** If it's only hidden in the client, it isn't hidden. 2. **Fail closed** — unknown/ambiguous → restricted, never exposed. 3. **Default to hide-value** so the tree's structure survives redaction. 4. **Make "who sees what" auditable** — owners must be able to see and preview it. ## References - PRD `5. Current State/3. Permissions and Roles`; existing tags + workspace-scoped RLS + permission tiers. - Linear: CVS-118 → CVS-123. Related: **CVS-93** (workspace settings, where groups are managed), **CVS-90** (connected accounts → source-derived access), **CVS-89** (API keys / MCP access must respect this too). Shell cwd was reset to /home/nadeemramli/workspace/github.com/nadeemramli/metric-mapping

content.text:
I have a complete picture now. Let me lay out the analysis before asking you to pick.

## What I found

**4 active agent worktrees (lanes), by actual file ownership:**

| Worktree | Lane | Owns (hot files) |
|---|---|---|
| `metrimap-strategy` | Strategy impact (CVS-166–171) | `features/strategy/*`, `strategyImpact.ts`, **canvas stores** (`useEdge/Node/GroupStore`), evidence, `editorjs-config`, `relationships.ts`, `types.ts` + a migration |
| `metrimap-integrations` | MCP/API + Linear (CVS-98–104, 24–28) | new MCP server, API/service layer, `services/*`, edge functions |
| `metrimap-home-settings` | Auth/Settings/DB-hardening (CVS-82, 64, 86, 87, 127) | `client.ts`, `settings/*`, cross-app hooks, RLS/advisor migrations |
| `metric-mapping` (this) | Evidence (CVS-34) | evidence pages — nearly done |

**Cold territory** (no worktree touching it):
- **Access & Visibility / node-level transparency** (CVS-118 spike → 119/120/121/122/123) — *entirely unowned.* New tables + RLS + a new service + node badge/settings UI. Direct continuation of the permission-tiers work I did before. Aligns with the north star ("govern the business value chain — safe to share the tree company-wide").
- **Canvas engine polish — Lane D** (CVS-165 dynamic edges [High/founder], CVS-43 grouping/subflows, CVS-40/77 realtime, CVS-44 edge routing). Unstarted, but funnels through `CanvasPage.tsx` + the 3 canvas stores — and the **strategy worktree is actively editing those stores** (small diffs, but real). The orchestration doc explicitly says Lane D = *one serial agent*.

**Key conflict insight:** the only truly conflict-free start is Access & Visibility — its first issues are a design spike (zero code) then *new* migration/service files. Canvas work would make me a de-facto second editor in the canvas stores the strategy lane holds.

Let me confirm the lane with you before I write the plan.

questions.question: Which lane should I take? All avoid the 3 other agents' hot files to different degrees.

options.label: Access & Visibility
options.description: CVS-118 spike → CVS-119 groups → CVS-120 access tags → CVS-121 RLS. Fully cold (no worktree owns permissions). Starts with a design doc + NEW tables/RLS/service files = zero conflict early. Continues my prior permission-tiers work; north-star aligned. Later UI touches settings/node cards (light overlap). Longer arc, high strategic value.

options.label: Canvas polish (Lane D)
options.description: e.g. CVS-165 dynamic relationship edges (High, founder request) or CVS-43 grouping+subflows. High product visibility, unstarted. BUT edits CanvasPage.tsx + canvas stores that the strategy worktree is also touching — needs rebase coordination; doc says Lane D should be one serial agent.

options.label: Canvas realtime fix
options.description: CVS-77 (updates/deletes lag one step behind — was a P1 correctness bug) + optionally CVS-40 realtime collab. Self-contained bugfix, but still in canvas store/realtime files that overlap the strategy lane.

options.label: Isolated small wins
options.description: Lower-risk self-contained items: CVS-83 global cross-canvas search, CVS-76 orphan metric-card auto-group, or CVS-135 toolbar-manifest follow-ups. Smaller value, still some canvas overlap.

content.text: Access & Visibility it is. Let me ground the plan in the actual codebase — how permissions/RLS, tags, and node/settings UI are currently built — so the plan reuses existing patterns.

input.description: Explore permissions and RLS
input.prompt:
Explore the metric-mapping repo at /home/nadeemramli/workspace/github.com/nadeemramli/metric-mapping to document the EXISTING permissions + RLS architecture, so we can extend it with node-level visibility (groups × access-tags).

Find and report, with file:line references and short code excerpts:
1. The permission-tiers implementation: search for `permission_tiers`, `my_project_permission`, `can_comment_project`, Viewer/Commenter/Editor/Admin roles. Where are these RPCs defined (which migration under supabase/migrations/)? Where are they called in the client (services/hooks/components)?
2. RLS structure: how are RLS policies written in supabase/migrations/? Find the migration(s) that add RLS to the `metric_cards`/`cards` table and `metric_values`. Quote a representative CREATE POLICY block so we know the style, the auth function used (e.g. requesting_user_id() / auth.jwt() / a Clerk helper), and how workspace/project scoping is done.
3. How `npm run test:rls` works: the test runner (scripts or src/tests), how a policy test is written, so a new visibility policy can add tests in the same style.
4. The Clerk→Supabase auth token path relevant to RLS (how the requesting user id is derived in policies).
5. The tables involved: schema of `metric_cards` (or `cards`), `metric_values`, `projects`, and any workspace/org table. Are there existing group/department tables? (there should NOT be — confirm.)

Return a structured findings report. Be precise about migration filenames and the exact SQL auth helper used in policies.

input.description: Explore tags system
input.prompt:
Explore the metric-mapping repo at /home/nadeemramli/workspace/github.com/nadeemramli/metric-mapping to document the EXISTING tags system, which we will extend so a tag can be an "access tag" carrying an audience (allowed groups).

Find and report with file:line references and short excerpts:
1. The tag tables: `tags`, `metric_card_tags`, `relationship_tags`. Which migration under supabase/migrations/ creates them? What columns do they have? Also note that `metric_cards` reportedly has a `tags[]` column — confirm and show it.
2. The TypeScript types for tags in src/shared/lib/supabase/types.ts (and any Prisma/Zod generated types in src/lib/prisma).
3. The service layer for tags: any file under src/shared/lib/supabase/services/ that does tag CRUD. How are tags created/assigned to a metric card?
4. The UI where a user assigns tags to a node/metric card (component files) — e.g. a card settings sheet, tag popover, or the CardSheet. Where would a "mark this tag as an access tag + pick audience" control naturally fit?
5. Any existing "tag" Zustand store or React Query hooks.

Return a structured findings report with exact filenames and the tag table column definitions.

content.text: I have everything I need. Writing the plan.

input.content:
# Lane: Access & Visibility (node-level transparency)

## Context

Four agent worktrees are already active and own distinct hot files:
- `metrimap-strategy` — Strategy impact; **owns the canvas stores** (`useNode/Edge/GroupStore`), evidence, `relationships.ts`, `types.ts`.
- `metrimap-integrations` — MCP server + API/service layer + edge functions.
- `metrimap-home-settings` — auth `client.ts`, `settings/*`, DB-hardening RLS (CVS-127).
- `metric-mapping` (here) — Evidence (CVS-34), nearly done.

The **Access & Visibility** project (Linear CVS-118 → 123, initiative *Platform, account & governance*) is **entirely unowned** — no worktree touches it. It's the natural continuation of my prior permission-tiers work and is north-star aligned: node-level visibility is what makes a company-wide driver tree safe to share (Finance sees all; Marketing sees their branch; nobody sees Net Profit who shouldn't — on the *same* canvas). Its first increments are a design spike + **new** migration/service files, so early work has **zero file-conflict** with the other lanes.

Vault design doc: `…/Canvasm - Easy Workflow/PRD/4. Product/4. Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency).md`.

## Model (from the vault doc)
`Viewer ∈ group(s)` × `node has access-tag → audience(groups)` → RLS decides: **show** / **hide-value** ("Restricted", default) / **hide-node** (node + edges omitted). RLS is the wall; UI is convenience. Fail closed.

## Lane setup (do first — avoid disrupting other lanes)
- Create a **dedicated worktree** so the cvs-34 working changes here are untouched and no two agents share a tree:
  `git worktree add ../metrimap-access -b <issue gitBranchName>` (branch per issue, off `main`).
- Everything below lands as **new files** where possible. The only shared-file edits are additive sections in `WorkspaceSettingsPage.tsx` (home-settings lane) and node card render — keep those diffs tight and **rebase on `main` before each PR**.

## Existing patterns to reuse (grounded in code)
- **RLS auth helper:** policies use `requesting_user_id()` (see `supabase/migrations/20260626120000_rls_clerk_hardening.sql`).
- **Permission RPC pattern:** `supabase/migrations/20260703180000_permission_tiers.sql` defines `my_project_permission(pid uuid)` / `can_comment_project(pid uuid)` as `SQL STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public`, granted to `authenticated, anon`. New visibility helpers follow this exact shape.
- **Client permission path:** `getMyProjectPermission` in `src/shared/lib/supabase/services/collaborators.ts` → `useCanvasPermission.ts` (`src/features/canvas/hooks/`). Mirror this for visibility.
- **Tags:** `tags`, `metric_card_tags`, `relationship_tags` (base schema `20250811100849_remote_schema.sql`; `metric_cards.tags text[]`; RLS on in `20260626120000`). Access-tags **extend** this — do not invent a parallel system.
- **Node UI plug-in points:** value render `src/features/canvas/components/nodes/metric-node/MetricCard.tsx` + `ValueNode.tsx`; card shell `src/features/canvas/components/primitives/NodeCardShell.tsx` (CVS-158) for the Restricted badge; per-node config sheet for the "Who can see this" control.
- **Filter point (hide-node/hide-edge):** the canvas load path via `useNodeStore` / `useEdgeStore` (coordinate: strategy lane also edits these — additive filter only, rebase).
- **RLS tests:** extend the `npm run test:rls` suite in the same style as the guest-tier tests.

## Execution sequence (branch + PR + manual-test sub-issue per issue)

**1. CVS-118 — Design spike (do first; zero code).** Post a "## Decision (locked)" comment on the issue settling: new `workspace_groups` + `group_members` (Clerk org stays the tenant boundary); access = audience-on-tag + per-node allowlist escape hatch; redaction default **hide-value**; info-leak policy = hide edges touching restricted nodes + derived-metric masking (strict default, configurable); data-source-derived access = **out of v1**; exact RLS predicates on `cards` + `metric_values`. Update the vault doc's "Decisions the spike settles" section.

**2. CVS-119 — Groups/departments (foundation).** New migration: `workspace_groups`, `group_members` (RLS-scoped via `requesting_user_id()`), plus a `my_groups()` `SECURITY DEFINER` helper. New service `src/shared/lib/supabase/services/groups.ts`; add types to `src/shared/lib/supabase/types.ts`. Admin UI: a **new** "Groups & Departments" section/route reachable from `WorkspaceSettingsPage.tsx` (create/rename group, add/remove members). Run `npm run prisma:types`.

**3. CVS-120 — Access tags + audience policy.** New migration: `tag_audiences` (tag → group) + `is_access` flag on tags; a `node_visible_to_me(card_id)` helper. Extend tag service + the per-node/CardSheet tag control to "mark as access tag + pick audience." No enforcement yet — model + assignment only.

**4. CVS-121 — RLS enforcement (the wall).** New migration adding policies on `metric_cards`/`cards`, relationships/edges, and `metric_values`: a row is readable only if `viewer groups ∩ node audience` (or owner/admin), else filtered (hide-node) or the value redacted (hide-value). Extend `test:rls` with group×audience cases (member sees / non-member filtered / value redacted / API path blocked). This is the security-critical PR — **do not self-merge** (per DB/security PR policy).

**5. CVS-122 — Redaction UX.** Per-node "Who can see this" + lock/**Restricted** badge on `NodeCardShell`; masked value ("Restricted") in `MetricCard`/`ValueNode`; admin access-matrix (tags × groups) + owner "view as <group>" preview. Client filter for hide-node in `useNode/EdgeStore` (additive).

**6. CVS-123 — Dashboard/canvas audience.** Audience picker on dashboards/shared views (`src/features/dashboard`), auto-filter to permitted nodes, "hides N restricted metrics" note; integrate with the existing share flow.

## Guardrails
- Branch-per-issue → PR → move issue to **In Review** (not Done) → create **Manual test** sub-issue assigned to the owner (`m.nadeemramli@gmail.com`, id `3f0b8049-…`).
- Verify each PR: `npm run type-check`, `npm run lint`, `npm run test`, and `npm run test:rls` (every issue here touches RLS). Never bypass Husky.
- **Do not self-merge** the RLS/DB PRs (CVS-119/120/121). Rebase on `main` before opening each PR; keep shared-file diffs (settings page, node render, stores) minimal and additive.

## Verification (end-to-end)
- **RLS is the real test:** after CVS-121, `npm run test:rls` must prove a Marketing-group user cannot read a `finance-only` node's row/value even via a direct client call (not just the UI).
- **Manual:** create two groups, tag a node finance-only, sign in as each group, confirm hide-value vs hide-node behavior on canvas + dashboard, and the "view as <group>" preview. Captured as the per-issue Manual test sub-issues.
- Start narrow: land CVS-118 (decisions) + CVS-119 (groups) first — both conflict-free — then reassess against the other lanes' progress before the RLS PR.

# Lane: Access & Visibility (node-level transparency)

## Context

Four agent worktrees are already active and own distinct hot files:
- `metrimap-strategy` — Strategy impact; **owns the canvas stores** (`useNode/Edge/GroupStore`), evidence, `relationships.ts`, `types.ts`.
- `metrimap-integrations` — MCP server + API/service layer + edge functions.
- `metrimap-home-settings` — auth `client.ts`, `settings/*`, DB-hardening RLS (CVS-127).
- `metric-mapping` (here) — Evidence (CVS-34), nearly done.

The **Access & Visibility** project (Linear CVS-118 → 123, initiative *Platform, account & governance*) is **entirely unowned** — no worktree touches it. It's the natural continuation of my prior permission-tiers work and is north-star aligned: node-level visibility is what makes a company-wide driver tree safe to share (Finance sees all; Marketing sees their branch; nobody sees Net Profit who shouldn't — on the *same* canvas). Its first increments are a design spike + **new** migration/service files, so early work has **zero file-conflict** with the other lanes.

Vault design doc: `…/Canvasm - Easy Workflow/PRD/4. Product/4. Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency).md`.

## Model (from the vault doc)
`Viewer ∈ group(s)` × `node has access-tag → audience(groups)` → RLS decides: **show** / **hide-value** ("Restricted", default) / **hide-node** (node + edges omitted). RLS is the wall; UI is convenience. Fail closed.

## Lane setup (do first — avoid disrupting other lanes)
- Create a **dedicated worktree** so the cvs-34 working changes here are untouched and no two agents share a tree:
  `git worktree add ../metrimap-access -b <issue gitBranchName>` (branch per issue, off `main`).
- Everything below lands as **new files** where possible. The only shared-file edits are additive sections in `WorkspaceSettingsPage.tsx` (home-settings lane) and node card render — keep those diffs tight and **rebase on `main` before each PR**.

## Existing patterns to reuse (grounded in code)
- **RLS auth helper:** policies use `requesting_user_id()` (see `supabase/migrations/20260626120000_rls_clerk_hardening.sql`).
- **Permission RPC pattern:** `supabase/migrations/20260703180000_permission_tiers.sql` defines `my_project_permission(pid uuid)` / `can_comment_project(pid uuid)` as `SQL STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public`, granted to `authenticated, anon`. New visibility helpers follow this exact shape.
- **Client permission path:** `getMyProjectPermission` in `src/shared/lib/supabase/services/collaborators.ts` → `useCanvasPermission.ts` (`src/features/canvas/hooks/`). Mirror this for visibility.
- **Tags:** `tags`, `metric_card_tags`, `relationship_tags` (base schema `20250811100849_remote_schema.sql`; `metric_cards.tags text[]`; RLS on in `20260626120000`). Access-tags **extend** this — do not invent a parallel system.
- **Node UI plug-in points:** value render `src/features/canvas/components/nodes/metric-node/MetricCard.tsx` + `ValueNode.tsx`; card shell `src/features/canvas/components/primitives/NodeCardShell.tsx` (CVS-158) for the Restricted badge; per-node config sheet for the "Who can see this" control.
- **Filter point (hide-node/hide-edge):** the canvas load path via `useNodeStore` / `useEdgeStore` (coordinate: strategy lane also edits these — additive filter only, rebase).
- **RLS tests:** extend the `npm run test:rls` suite in the same style as the guest-tier tests.

## Execution sequence (branch + PR + manual-test sub-issue per issue)

**1. CVS-118 — Design spike (do first; zero code).** Post a "## Decision (locked)" comment on the issue settling: new `workspace_groups` + `group_members` (Clerk org stays the tenant boundary); access = audience-on-tag + per-node allowlist escape hatch; redaction default **hide-value**; info-leak policy = hide edges touching restricted nodes + derived-metric masking (strict default, configurable); data-source-derived access = **out of v1**; exact RLS predicates on `cards` + `metric_values`. Update the vault doc's "Decisions the spike settles" section.

**2. CVS-119 — Groups/departments (foundation).** New migration: `workspace_groups`, `group_members` (RLS-scoped via `requesting_user_id()`), plus a `my_groups()` `SECURITY DEFINER` helper. New service `src/shared/lib/supabase/services/groups.ts`; add types to `src/shared/lib/supabase/types.ts`. Admin UI: a **new** "Groups & Departments" section/route reachable from `WorkspaceSettingsPage.tsx` (create/rename group, add/remove members). Run `npm run prisma:types`.

**3. CVS-120 — Access tags + audience policy.** New migration: `tag_audiences` (tag → group) + `is_access` flag on tags; a `node_visible_to_me(card_id)` helper. Extend tag service + the per-node/CardSheet tag control to "mark as access tag + pick audience." No enforcement yet — model + assignment only.

**4. CVS-121 — RLS enforcement (the wall).** New migration adding policies on `metric_cards`/`cards`, relationships/edges, and `metric_values`: a row is readable only if `viewer groups ∩ node audience` (or owner/admin), else filtered (hide-node) or the value redacted (hide-value). Extend `test:rls` with group×audience cases (member sees / non-member filtered / value redacted / API path blocked). This is the security-critical PR — **do not self-merge** (per DB/security PR policy).

**5. CVS-122 — Redaction UX.** Per-node "Who can see this" + lock/**Restricted** badge on `NodeCardShell`; masked value ("Restricted") in `MetricCard`/`ValueNode`; admin access-matrix (tags × groups) + owner "view as <group>" preview. Client filter for hide-node in `useNode/EdgeStore` (additive).

**6. CVS-123 — Dashboard/canvas audience.** Audience picker on dashboards/shared views (`src/features/dashboard`), auto-filter to permitted nodes, "hides N restricted metrics" note; integrate with the existing share flow.

## Guardrails
- Branch-per-issue → PR → move issue to **In Review** (not Done) → create **Manual test** sub-issue assigned to the owner (`m.nadeemramli@gmail.com`, id `3f0b8049-…`).
- Verify each PR: `npm run type-check`, `npm run lint`, `npm run test`, and `npm run test:rls` (every issue here touches RLS). Never bypass Husky.
- **Do not self-merge** the RLS/DB PRs (CVS-119/120/121). Rebase on `main` before opening each PR; keep shared-file diffs (settings page, node render, stores) minimal and additive.

## Verification (end-to-end)
- **RLS is the real test:** after CVS-121, `npm run test:rls` must prove a Marketing-group user cannot read a `finance-only` node's row/value even via a direct client call (not just the UI).
- **Manual:** create two groups, tag a node finance-only, sign in as each group, confirm hide-value vs hide-node behavior on canvas + dashboard, and the "view as <group>" preview. Captured as the per-issue Manual test sub-issues.
- Start narrow: land CVS-118 (decisions) + CVS-119 (groups) first — both conflict-free — then reassess against the other lanes' progress before the RLS PR.

- **Client permission path:** `getMyProjectPermission` in `src/shared/lib/supabase/services/collaborators.ts` → `useCanvasPermission.ts` (`src/features/canvas/hooks/`). Mirror this for visibility.
- **Tags:** `tags`, `metric_card_tags`, `relationship_tags` (base schema `20250811100849_remote_schema.sql`; `metric_cards.tags text[]`; RLS on in `20260626120000`). Access-tags **extend** this — do not invent a parallel system.
- **Node UI plug-in points:** value render `src/features/canvas/components/nodes/metric-node/MetricCard.tsx` + `ValueNode.tsx`; card shell `src/features/canvas/components/primitives/NodeCardShell.tsx` (CVS-158) for the Restricted badge; per-node config sheet for the "Who can see this" control.
- **Filter point (hide-node/hide-edge):** the canvas load path via `useNodeStore` / `useEdgeStore` (coordinate: strategy lane also edits these — additive filter only, rebase).
- **RLS tests:** extend the `npm run test:rls` suite in the same style as the guest-tier tests.

## Execution sequence (branch + PR + manual-test sub-issue per issue)

**1. CVS-118 — Design spike (do first; zero code).** Post a "## Decision (locked)" comment on the issue settling: new `workspace_groups` + `group_members` (Clerk org stays the tenant boundary); access = audience-on-tag + per-node allowlist escape hatch; redaction default **hide-value**; info-leak policy = hide edges touching restricted nodes + derived-metric masking (strict default, configurable); data-source-derived access = **out of v1**; exact RLS predicates on `cards` + `metric_values`. Update the vault doc's "Decisions the spike settles" section.

**2. CVS-119 — Groups/departments (foundation).** New migration: `workspace_groups`, `group_members` (RLS-scoped via `requesting_user_id()`), plus a `my_groups()` `SECURITY DEFINER` helper. New service `src/shared/lib/supabase/services/groups.ts`; add types to `src/shared/lib/supabase/types.ts`. Admin UI: a **new** "Groups & Departments" section/route reachable from `WorkspaceSettingsPage.tsx` (create/rename group, add/remove members). Run `npm run prisma:types`.

**3. CVS-120 — Access tags + audience policy.** New migration: `tag_audiences` (tag → group) + `is_access` flag on tags; a `node_visible_to_me(card_id)` helper. Extend tag service + the per-node/CardSheet tag control to "mark as access tag + pick audience." No enforcement yet — model + assignment only.

**4. CVS-121 — RLS enforcement (the wall).** New migration adding policies on `metric_cards`/`cards`, relationships/edges, and `metric_values`: a row is readable only if `viewer groups ∩ node audience` (or owner/admin), else filtered (hide-node) or the value redacted (hide-value). Extend `test:rls` with group×audience cases (member sees / non-member filtered / value redacted / API path blocked). This is the security-critical PR — **do not self-merge** (per DB/security PR policy).

**5. CVS-122 — Redaction UX.** Per-node "Who can see this" + lock/**Restricted** badge on `NodeCardShell`; masked value ("Restricted") in `MetricCard`/`ValueNode`; admin access-matrix (tags × groups) + owner "view as <group>" preview. Client filter for hide-node in `useNode/EdgeStore` (additive).

**6. CVS-123 — Dashboard/canvas audience.** Audience picker on dashboards/shared views (`src/features/dashboard`), auto-filter to permitted nodes, "hides N restricted metrics" note; integrate with the existing share flow.

## Guardrails
- Branch-per-issue → PR → move issue to **In Review** (not Done) → create **Manual test** sub-issue assigned to the owner (`m.nadeemramli@gmail.com`, id `3f0b8049-…`).
- Verify each PR: `npm run type-check`, `npm run lint`, `npm run test`, and `npm run test:rls` (every issue here touches RLS). Never bypass Husky.
- **Do not self-merge** the RLS/DB PRs (CVS-119/120/121). Rebase on `main` before opening each PR; keep shared-file diffs (settings page, node render, stores) minimal and additive.

## Verification (end-to-end)
- **RLS is the real test:** after CVS-121, `npm run test:rls` must prove a Marketing-group user cannot read a `finance-only` node's row/value even via a direct client call (not just the UI).
- **Manual:** create two groups, tag a node finance-only, sign in as each group, confirm hide-value vs hide-node behavior on canvas + dashboard, and the "view as <group>" preview. Captured as the per-issue Manual test sub-issues.
- Start narrow: land CVS-118 (decisions) + CVS-119 (groups) first — both conflict-free — then reassess against the other lanes' progress before the RLS PR.

content.text:
{"id":"CVS-118","title":"[Spike] Design: node-level visibility model (tags × groups × RLS)","description":"**Design spike — do first.** Settle the model + the hard decisions before building.

## Decisions to make

* **Group model:** new `groups`/`department` membership within a workspace vs. extend Clerk org roles. (Current permission tiers only gate outside guests — no internal tiering.)
* **Access-tag model:** reuse the existing `tags` / `metric_card_tags` — mark a tag as an *access tag* with an **audience** (allowed groups). Per-node allowlist as an escape hatch?
* **Redaction mode:** support **hide-value** (structure stays, number → "Restricted") and **hide-node** (node + edges vanish); default = hide-value.
* **Info-leak policy (the hard one):** hide restricted nodes' **edges** from unauthorized viewers; decide whether metrics **derived from** restricted inputs are auto-masked.
* **Data-source-derived access:** should a metric sourced from a restricted connection (e.g. accounting) inherit restriction automatically?
* **Enforcement:** exact RLS predicates on `cards` + `metric_values` keyed on viewer group ∩ node access-tag audience.

## Deliverable

Short design doc in the vault + the answers above so issues below can proceed.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Group + access-tag model chosen
- [ ] Redaction modes + default decided
- [ ] Info-leak + derived-metric policy decided

content.content: Task #7 created successfully: CVS-123 — Dashboard/canvas audience
Updated task #1 status

1	-- =====================================================================
2	-- PERMISSION TIERS — Viewer / Commenter / Editor / Admin (Metrimap)
3	-- Model: "org = collaborative" — every active-workspace (Clerk org) member keeps
4	-- full write (unchanged). Per-project roles constrain EXTERNAL/guest
5	-- collaborators (project_collaborators rows) and public links.
6	--
7	--   viewer    = read only
8	--   commenter = read + comment (NOT edit)   <- new tier
9	--   editor    = read + comment + edit        <- role now live in the CHECK
10	--   admin     = editor + (member mgmt is owner-only elsewhere)
11	--
12	-- has_project_access(pid, true) is unchanged (editor/admin/owner or edit/write/
13	-- admin token => write). Adds a separate can_comment_project so commenting no
14	-- longer requires full write, and my_project_permission for authoritative UI
15	-- gating that mirrors these helpers.
16	-- =====================================================================
17	BEGIN;
18	
19	-- 1. Allow the new roles. Keep legacy 'member' for existing rows.
20	ALTER TABLE public.project_collaborators
21	  DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS project_collaborators_role_check;
22	ALTER TABLE public.project_collaborators
23	  ADD CONSTRAINT project_collaborators_role_check
24	  CHECK (role = ANY (ARRAY['owner','admin','editor','commenter','member','viewer']));
25	
26	-- 2. Comment access: view + (org member / null-workspace creator / collaborator
27	--    with a comment-or-higher role or token). Commenters get this but NOT write.
28	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.can_comment_project(pid uuid)
29	RETURNS boolean
30	LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
31	AS $$
32	  SELECT (public.requesting_user_id() IS NOT NULL) AND EXISTS (
33	    SELECT 1 FROM public.projects p
34	    WHERE p.id = pid AND (
35	      (public.requesting_org_id() IS NOT NULL AND p.workspace_id = public.requesting_org_id())
36	      OR (p.workspace_id IS NULL AND p.created_by = public.requesting_user_id())
37	      OR EXISTS (
38	        SELECT 1 FROM public.project_collaborators pc
39	        WHERE pc.project_id = p.id AND pc.user_id = public.requesting_user_id()
40	          AND (pc.role = ANY (ARRAY['owner','admin','editor','commenter'])
41	            OR pc.permissions @> ARRAY['comment']::text[]
42	            OR pc.permissions @> ARRAY['edit']::text[]
43	            OR pc.permissions @> ARRAY['write']::text[]
44	            OR pc.permissions @> ARRAY['admin']::text[])
45	      )
46	    )
47	  );
48	$$;
49	
50	-- 3. Repoint comment INSERT policies from write-gate to comment-gate. (UPDATE/
51	--    DELETE stay write-gated — resolving/removing threads is an editor action.)
52	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS comment_threads_insert ON public.comment_threads;
53	CREATE POLICY comment_threads_insert ON public.comment_threads
54	  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (public.can_comment_project(project_id));
55	
56	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS comments_insert ON public.comments;
57	CREATE POLICY comments_insert ON public.comments
58	  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (EXISTS (
59	    SELECT 1 FROM public.comment_threads t
60	    WHERE t.id = comments.thread_id AND public.can_comment_project(t.project_id)
61	  ));
62	
63	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS comment_mentions_insert ON public.comment_mentions;
64	CREATE POLICY comment_mentions_insert ON public.comment_mentions
65	  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (EXISTS (
66	    SELECT 1 FROM public.comments c
67	    JOIN public.comment_threads t ON t.id = c.thread_id
68	    WHERE c.id = comment_mentions.comment_id
69	      AND public.can_comment_project(t.project_id)
70	  ));
71	
72	-- 4. Authoritative effective-permission for the current user on a project, so
73	--    UI gating exactly mirrors RLS. 'none' | 'view' | 'comment' | 'edit'.
74	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.my_project_permission(pid uuid)
75	RETURNS text
76	LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
77	AS $$
78	  SELECT CASE
79	    WHEN NOT public.can_view_project(pid) THEN 'none'
80	    WHEN public.has_project_access(pid, true) THEN 'edit'
81	    WHEN public.can_comment_project(pid) THEN 'comment'
82	    ELSE 'view'
83	  END;
84	$$;
85	GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.my_project_permission(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;
86	
87	COMMIT;
88

-- =====================================================================
-- PERMISSION TIERS — Viewer / Commenter / Editor / Admin (Metrimap)
-- Model: "org = collaborative" — every active-workspace (Clerk org) member keeps
-- full write (unchanged). Per-project roles constrain EXTERNAL/guest
-- collaborators (project_collaborators rows) and public links.
--
--   viewer    = read only
--   commenter = read + comment (NOT edit)   <- new tier
--   editor    = read + comment + edit        <- role now live in the CHECK
--   admin     = editor + (member mgmt is owner-only elsewhere)
--
-- has_project_access(pid, true) is unchanged (editor/admin/owner or edit/write/
-- admin token => write). Adds a separate can_comment_project so commenting no
-- longer requires full write, and my_project_permission for authoritative UI
-- gating that mirrors these helpers.
-- =====================================================================
BEGIN;

-- 1. Allow the new roles. Keep legacy 'member' for existing rows.
ALTER TABLE public.project_collaborators
  DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS project_collaborators_role_check;
ALTER TABLE public.project_collaborators
  ADD CONSTRAINT project_collaborators_role_check
  CHECK (role = ANY (ARRAY['owner','admin','editor','commenter','member','viewer']));

-- 2. Comment access: view + (org member / null-workspace creator / collaborator
--    with a comment-or-higher role or token). Commenters get this but NOT write.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.can_comment_project(pid uuid)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT (public.requesting_user_id() IS NOT NULL) AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.projects p
    WHERE p.id = pid AND (
      (public.requesting_org_id() IS NOT NULL AND p.workspace_id = public.requesting_org_id())
      OR (p.workspace_id IS NULL AND p.created_by = public.requesting_user_id())
      OR EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.project_collaborators pc
        WHERE pc.project_id = p.id AND pc.user_id = public.requesting_user_id()
          AND (pc.role = ANY (ARRAY['owner','admin','editor','commenter'])
            OR pc.permissions @> ARRAY['comment']::text[]
            OR pc.permissions @> ARRAY['edit']::text[]
            OR pc.permissions @> ARRAY['write']::text[]
            OR pc.permissions @> ARRAY['admin']::text[])
      )
    )
  );
$$;

-- 3. Repoint comment INSERT policies from write-gate to comment-gate. (UPDATE/
--    DELETE stay write-gated — resolving/removing threads is an editor action.)
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS comment_threads_insert ON public.comment_threads;
CREATE POLICY comment_threads_insert ON public.comment_threads
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (public.can_comment_project(project_id));

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS comments_insert ON public.comments;
CREATE POLICY comments_insert ON public.comments
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.comment_threads t
    WHERE t.id = comments.thread_id AND public.can_comment_project(t.project_id)
  ));

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS comment_mentions_insert ON public.comment_mentions;
CREATE POLICY comment_mentions_insert ON public.comment_mentions
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.comments c
    JOIN public.comment_threads t ON t.id = c.thread_id
    WHERE c.id = comment_mentions.comment_id
      AND public.can_comment_project(t.project_id)
  ));

-- 4. Authoritative effective-permission for the current user on a project, so
--    UI gating exactly mirrors RLS. 'none' | 'view' | 'comment' | 'edit'.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.my_project_permission(pid uuid)
RETURNS text
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT CASE
    WHEN NOT public.can_view_project(pid) THEN 'none'
    WHEN public.has_project_access(pid, true) THEN 'edit'
    WHEN public.can_comment_project(pid) THEN 'comment'
    ELSE 'view'
  END;
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.my_project_permission(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

COMMIT;

content.content:
1	-- =====================================================================
2	-- DATA LIFECYCLE (B.2) — shared value store for Tracked Metrics.
3	-- One row per (tracked_metric_id, period) = the current series, so every card
4	-- referencing the metric reads ONE shared history. Owner-scoped (created_by).
5	-- Versioning / time-travel comes later via a separate append-only history table.
6	-- See docs/backlog/object-model-and-catalog.md. APPLIED to prod 2026-06-29.
7	-- =====================================================================
8	BEGIN;
9	
10	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.metric_values (
11	  id                uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
12	  tracked_metric_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES public.tracked_metrics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
13	  period            text NOT NULL,
14	  value             double precision NOT NULL,
15	  change_percent    double precision,
16	  trend             text,
17	  source            text,
18	  created_by        text NOT NULL DEFAULT public.requesting_user_id(),
19	  updated_at        timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
20	  UNIQUE (tracked_metric_id, period)
21	);
22	
23	ALTER TABLE public.metric_values ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
24	
25	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS metric_values_select ON public.metric_values;
26	CREATE POLICY metric_values_select ON public.metric_values
27	  FOR SELECT USING (created_by = public.requesting_user_id());
28	
29	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS metric_values_insert ON public.metric_values;
30	CREATE POLICY metric_values_insert ON public.metric_values
31	  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (created_by = public.requesting_user_id());
32	
33	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS metric_values_update ON public.metric_values;
34	CREATE POLICY metric_values_update ON public.metric_values
35	  FOR UPDATE USING (created_by = public.requesting_user_id())
36	  WITH CHECK (created_by = public.requesting_user_id());
37	
38	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS metric_values_delete ON public.metric_values;
39	CREATE POLICY metric_values_delete ON public.metric_values
40	  FOR DELETE USING (created_by = public.requesting_user_id());
41	
42	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_metric_values_tracked_metric_id
43	  ON public.metric_values(tracked_metric_id);
44	
45	COMMIT;
46

content.text: Schema confirmed. Now I'll post the locked-decision comment on CVS-118 — grounded in the real helpers (`requesting_user_id()`, `requesting_org_id()`, `has_project_access`) and the real tables, and resolving the founder's CVS-136 proxy questions.

input.body:
## Decision (locked)

Design settled against the current schema (`requesting_user_id()` / `requesting_org_id()` RLS helpers; `has_project_access(pid,true)` / `can_view_project(pid)` / `my_project_permission(pid)` from `20260703180000_permission_tiers.sql`; `tags`, `metric_card_tags`, `metric_cards(project_id, owner_id, created_by)`, `metric_values(tracked_metric_id, created_by)`). Vault doc `PRD/4. Product/4. Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency)` is the shaped source; this comment is the build contract.

### 1. Group model — NEW tables (not Clerk roles)
- `workspace_groups(id uuid, workspace_id text, name text, created_by text, …)` scoped by `workspace_id = requesting_org_id()`; `group_members(group_id uuid, user_id text)`. A user belongs to 1+.
- Clerk org stays the **tenant boundary**; groups add the **within-workspace tiering** that doesn't exist today (org members currently all get full edit).
- Helper: `my_groups()` → `setof uuid`, `SQL STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public`, returns the requester's group ids in the current org. Granted to `authenticated, anon`.

### 2. Access-tag model — extend the existing tag system
- Add `is_access boolean default false` to `tags`; new `tag_audiences(tag_id uuid, group_id uuid)` = which groups may see nodes carrying that tag.
- **Escape hatch:** per-node allowlist `node_access_grants(metric_card_id uuid, group_id uuid)` for one-off overrides. Tag-audience is the norm; allowlist is the exception.
- **No access tag on a node ⇒ visible to everyone with canvas access** (unchanged behaviour).

### 3. Redaction modes — `hide-value` default
- Mode stored per access-tag (`tags.redaction_mode text check in ('hide_value','hide_node') default 'hide_value'`), node-level override allowed later.
- **hide-value (default):** node + edges stay; the number/series renders **"Restricted"** — preserves tree shape.
- **hide-node:** node + its incident edges omitted for unauthorized viewers.

### 4. Enforcement — RLS is the wall, keyed on `my_groups() ∩ node audience`
- Central helper `node_visible_to_me(card_id uuid) → boolean` (`SECURITY DEFINER`, **fail closed**): true if the card has no access tag, OR requester has `has_project_access(project_id,true)` (owner/admin/editor), OR an access tag on the card has `tag_audiences ∩ my_groups() ≠ ∅`, OR a `node_access_grants` row matches.
- `metric_cards` SELECT policy: visible when `node_visible_to_me(id)` **or** the tag's mode is `hide_value` (row stays, value redacted). Rows with a `hide_node` tag the viewer can't see are filtered out.
- **Edges:** `relationships`/edge SELECT filters any edge whose source or target card is hide_node-restricted for the viewer (baseline info-leak defense).
- **Values (the subtlety):** `metric_values` is keyed on `tracked_metric_id` and currently owner-scoped by `created_by`. A tracked metric can back multiple cards, so value redaction is resolved **per-card at the value-for-card boundary**: a value is shown for a card only if `node_visible_to_me(card_id)`. Keep the existing owner-scoped `metric_values` RLS and add the card-audience gate at the resolver/policy join `metric_values → tracked_metrics → metric_cards`. Exact predicate finalized in CVS-121; **default deny**.
- All helpers granted to `authenticated, anon`; mirror the client via a `node_visible_to_me` batch RPC feeding a `useNodeVisibility` hook (same shape as `getMyProjectPermission → useCanvasPermission`).

### 5. Info-leak / derived-metric policy
- Ship **edge-hiding for hide_node** in v1 (baseline).
- **Derived-metric masking:** configurable; **default strict for one hop** — a metric directly computed from a restricted input is masked. Deeper transitive masking is a follow-up (note in CVS-121), not v1-blocking.

### 6. Data-source-derived access — OUT of v1
- A metric inheriting restriction from a restricted source connection is deferred to when **Connected accounts (CVS-90)** lands; then a connection carries an audience its bound metrics inherit. Tracked as v2.

### 7. Proxy departments / scopes (CVS-136) — resolution
A **proxy is a canvas/project-scoped named alias** (Finance, Exec, Board View), a **UX convenience layer, not a separate RLS axis**:
- **(a) alias over both** workspace groups and access tags — a proxy maps to a set of groups and applies an access tag/audience when assigned.
- **(b)** dashboards, canvas groups, and individual nodes may each carry a proxy assignment.
- **(c)** inheritance dashboard → canvas-group → node, **most-specific wins**; a node's own audience overrides inherited.
- **(d)** proxy assignment **resolves into access tags/groups first**; RLS only ever evaluates `my_groups() ∩ audience`. Proxies never touch RLS directly.
- **Scope:** proxy layer is **v2**, after the core (119→123). CVS-136 stays blocked by this spike and is scheduled after CVS-121.

### v1 build scope
CVS-119 groups → CVS-120 access tags + audience (`is_access`, `tag_audiences`, `node_access_grants`, `redaction_mode`) → CVS-121 RLS (`my_groups`, `node_visible_to_me`, cards/edges/values policies, `test:rls`) → CVS-122 redaction UX (Restricted badge on `NodeCardShell`, masked value in `MetricCard`/`ValueNode`, admin matrix, view-as-group) → CVS-123 dashboard audience.
**Deferred (v2):** transitive derived-metric masking, data-source-derived access (CVS-90), proxy departments (CVS-136).

### Principles
RLS is the boundary, UI is convenience · fail closed · default hide-value so structure survives · make "who sees what" auditable (`visibility_audit`).

✅ Acceptance criteria met: group + access-tag model chosen · redaction modes + default decided · info-leak + derived-metric policy decided · RLS predicate approach defined · CVS-136 proxy questions answered.
