{"id":"7eab56f1-405c-4b0b-abce-685d07923dec","body":"## Decision (locked)

Design settled against the current schema (`requesting_user_id()` / `requesting_org_id()` RLS helpers; `has_project_access(pid,true)` / `can_view_project(pid)` / `my_project_permission(pid)` from `20260703180000_permission_tiers.sql`; `tags`, `metric_card_tags`, `metric_cards(project_id, owner_id, created_by)`, `metric_values(tracked_metric_id, created_by)`). Vault doc `PRD/4. Product/4. Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency)` is the shaped source; this comment is the build contract.

### 1. Group model — NEW tables (not Clerk roles)
- `workspace_groups(id uuid, workspace_id text, name text, created_by text, …)` scoped by `workspace_id = requesting_org_id()`; `group_members(group_id uuid, user_id text)`. A user belongs to 1+.
- Clerk org stays the **tenant boundary**; groups add the **within-workspace tiering** that doesn't exist today (org members currently all get full edit).
- Helper: `my_groups()` → `setof uuid`, `SQL STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public`, returns the requester's group ids in the current org. Granted to `authenticated, anon`.

### 2. Access-tag model — extend the existing tag system
- Add `is_access boolean default false` to `tags`; new `tag_audiences(tag_id uuid, group_id uuid)` = which groups may see nodes carrying that tag.
- **Escape hatch:** per-node allowlist `node_access_grants(metric_card_id uuid, group_id uuid)` for one-off overrides. Tag-audience is the norm; allowlist is the exception.
- **No access tag on a node ⇒ visible to everyone with canvas access** (unchanged behaviour).

### 3. Redaction modes — `hide-value` default
- Mode stored per access-tag (`tags.redaction_mode text check in ('hide_value','hide_node') default 'hide_value'`), node-level override allowed later.
- **hide-value (default):** node + edges stay; the number/series renders **"Restricted"** — preserves tree shape.
- **hide-node:** node + its incident edges omitted for unauthorized viewers.

### 4. Enforcement — RLS is the wall, keyed on `my_groups() ∩ node audience`
- Central helper `node_visible_to_me(card_id uuid) → boolean` (`SECURITY DEFINER`, **fail closed**): true if the card has no access tag, OR requester has `has_project_access(project_id,true)` (owner/admin/editor), OR an access tag on the card has `tag_audiences ∩ my_groups() ≠ ∅`, OR a `node_access_grants` row matches.
- `metric_cards` SELECT policy: visible when `node_visible_to_me(id)` **or** the tag's mode is `hide_value` (row stays, value redacted). Rows with a `hide_node` tag the viewer can't see are filtered out.
- **Edges:** `relationships`/edge SELECT filters any edge whose source or target card is hide_node-restricted for the viewer (baseline info-leak defense).
- **Values (the subtlety):** `metric_values` is keyed on `tracked_metric_id` and currently owner-scoped by `created_by`. A tracked metric can back multiple cards, so value redaction is resolved **per-card at the value-for-card boundary**: a value is shown for a card only if `node_visible_to_me(card_id)`. Keep the existing owner-scoped `metric_values` RLS and add the card-audience gate at the resolver/policy join `metric_values → tracked_metrics → metric_cards`. Exact predicate finalized in CVS-121; **default deny**.
- All helpers granted to `authenticated, anon`; mirror the client via a `node_visible_to_me` batch RPC feeding a `useNodeVisibility` hook (same shape as `getMyProjectPermission → useCanvasPermission`).

### 5. Info-leak / derived-metric policy
- Ship **edge-hiding for hide_node** in v1 (baseline).
- **Derived-metric masking:** configurable; **default strict for one hop** — a metric directly computed from a restricted input is masked. Deeper transitive masking is a follow-up (note in CVS-121), not v1-blocking.

### 6. Data-source-derived access — OUT of v1
- A metric inheriting restriction from a restricted source connection is deferred to when **Connected accounts (CVS-90)** lands; then a connection carries an audience its bound metrics inherit. Tracked as v2.

### 7. Proxy departments / scopes (CVS-136) — resolution
A **proxy is a canvas/project-scoped named alias** (Finance, Exec, Board View), a **UX convenience layer, not a separate RLS axis**:
- **(a) alias over both** workspace groups and access tags — a proxy maps to a set of groups and applies an access tag/audience when assigned.
- **(b)** dashboards, canvas groups, and individual nodes may each carry a proxy assignment.
- **(c)** inheritance dashboard → canvas-group → node, **most-specific wins**; a node's own audience overrides inherited.
- **(d)** proxy assignment **resolves into access tags/groups first**; RLS only ever evaluates `my_groups() ∩ audience`. Proxies never touch RLS directly.
- **Scope:** proxy layer is **v2**, after the core (119→123). CVS-136 stays blocked by this spike and is scheduled after CVS-121.

### v1 build scope
CVS-119 groups → CVS-120 access tags + audience (`is_access`, `tag_audiences`, `node_access_grants`, `redaction_mode`) → CVS-121 RLS (`my_groups`, `node_visible_to_me`, cards/edges/values policies, `test:rls`) → CVS-122 redaction UX (Restricted badge on `NodeCardShell`, masked value in `MetricCard`/`ValueNode`, admin matrix, view-as-group) → CVS-123 dashboard audience.
**Deferred (v2):** transitive derived-metric masking, data-source-derived access (CVS-90), proxy departments (CVS-136).

### Principles
RLS is the boundary, UI is convenience · fail closed · default hide-value so structure survives · make "who sees what" auditable (`visibility_audit`).

✅ Acceptance criteria met: group + access-tag model chosen · redaction modes + default decided · info-leak + derived-metric policy decided · RLS predicate approach defined · CVS-136 proxy questions answered.","createdAt":"2026-07-04T16:44:15.300Z","updatedAt":"2026-07-04T16:44:15.134Z","parentId":null,"resolvedAt":null,"quotedText":null,"author":{"id":"3f0b8049-7811-47da-93f8-5d93770d4348","name":"Nadeem Ramli"}}

- Access-tag model: audience on a tag vs. per-node allowlist (support both; tag is the norm, allowlist the escape hatch).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
--- draft: false title: "Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency)" date: "2026-07-04" tags: - canvasm - product - permissions - security - rls - design --- # Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency) — Design Design doc for **node-level access control** on the canvas. Shaped output of the design spike (Linear **CVS-118**) so the build issues **CVS-119 → CVS-123** can proceed. Project: *Access & visibility (node-level transparency)*. ## Why this matters The driver tree is meant to be the **shared source of truth that drives the whole team** — everyone reasons off the same map. But **not everything can be shown to everyone**: Net Profit, margins, payroll, CAC/LTV by channel, revenue by customer. Without granular visibility a business owner is stuck in a lose-lose: - **over-share** → leak sensitive numbers to people who shouldn't see them, or - **don't share** → the tree stops being a team tool and reverts to a private artifact. **Node-level visibility is what makes a company-wide driver tree safe to share.** Finance sees the whole picture; Marketing sees their branch; nobody sees Net Profit who shouldn't — on the *same* canvas. ## Current state (what we build on) - **Org-collaborative permissions** (PRD `5. Current State/3. Permissions and Roles`): workspace = Clerk org; **all members get full edit**. Roles (Viewer / Commenter / Editor / Admin) only gate **outside guests**. **No within-workspace tiering yet** — so today Finance and Marketing in the same workspace see everything. - **Tags already exist:** `tags`, `metric_card_tags`, `relationship_tags`; cards carry `tags[]`. - **RLS** is workspace-scoped and Clerk-hardened; `permission_tiers` + `metric_values` migrations exist; `npm run test:rls` covers the guest tiers. So this **extends** the model — it doesn't invent a new one. ## The model — tag × group, enforced at RLS ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Viewer (member of Marketing)"] -->|reads canvas| R["RLS on cards + metric_values"] R -->|viewer's groups ∩ node audience| D{"Allowed?"} D -- yes --> V["Show node + value"] D -- no (hide-value) --> M["Show node, value = 'Restricted'"] D -- no (hide-node) --> H["Node + its edges omitted"] N["Net Profit node
access tag: finance-only"] --> R ``` 1. **Groups / departments (new):** groups *within* a workspace (Finance, Marketing, Exec); a user belongs to 1+. Introduces internal tiering that doesn't exist today. 2. **Access tags with an audience:** reuse the existing tag system — a tag can be marked an **access tag** carrying an **audience** (allowed groups). Assign it when creating/editing a node (and on dashboards). **No access tag = visible to everyone with canvas access.** 3. **Two redaction modes** (per node / policy): - **Hide value** (default): node + relationships stay, the number/series shows **"Restricted"** — preserves the tree's shape so the map still reads. - **Hide node**: node + its edges are omitted for unauthorized viewers — for truly secret items. 4. **RLS is the hard boundary:** policies on `cards`/nodes and `metric_values` check the viewer's **group membership ∩ node access-tag audience**. Unauthorized rows are filtered (hide-node) or value-redacted (hide-value). **A marketer can't fetch Net Profit even via the API or MCP.** The UI is convenience; RLS is the wall. 5. **Dashboard / canvas audience:** assign an audience to a dashboard or shared view so it only surfaces permitted metrics; the existing share flow (People tab / roles) honors node visibility. ## The hard problem — information leakage A restricted node that **drives** a visible metric can leak through the **edge** (its existence/label) or the **derived value** (you can back it out). This is the real design call: - Hide **edges** touching restricted nodes from unauthorized viewers (baseline). - Decide whether metrics **derived from** restricted inputs are **also masked** — strict (mask anything downstream of a secret) vs. permissive (show the output, hide the input). Recommend **configurable**, default strict for anything one hop from a restricted input. ## Enhancement — access derived from the data source From the motivating example (an accounting connection only accountants can reach): a metric **sourced from a restricted connection** could **inherit** restriction automatically — access follows the data source. Cleaner than tagging each node by hand. (Ties to Connected accounts, CVS-90.) ## Decisions the spike settles - Group model: new `workspace_groups` + `group_members` vs. extend Clerk roles. (Recommend new groups; Clerk org stays the tenant boundary.) - Access-tag model: audience on a tag vs. per-node allowlist (support both; tag is the norm, allowlist the escape hatch). - Redaction default: **hide-value**. - Info-leak policy: edge hiding + derived-metric masking (strict default, configurable). - Data-source-derived access: in or out of v1. - Exact RLS predicates on `cards` + `metric_values`. ## Data-model additions - `workspace_groups`, `group_members` (RLS-scoped). - Access-tag audience: `tag_audiences` (tag → group) or an `is_access` + audience on tags. - Node redaction mode (per node/tag). - RLS policies on `cards`, edges/relationships, `metric_values`. - `visibility_audit` (who set what audience, when). ## UX - Per-node **"Who can see this"** control + a **lock / Restricted badge**. - Redacted rendering for unauthorized viewers (masked value or omitted node). - Admin **access matrix** (access tags × groups) + **"view as "** preview for owners. - Dashboard **audience picker** + "hides N restricted metrics for this audience". ## Phased plan (Linear) `CVS-118` spike (this) → **`CVS-119`** groups · **`CVS-120`** access tags + policy → **`CVS-121`** RLS enforcement → **`CVS-122`** redaction UX + admin matrix · **`CVS-123`** dashboard audience. Critical path: spike → (groups + tags) → RLS → (UX + dashboard). ## Principles 1. **RLS is the boundary; UI is convenience.** If it's only hidden in the client, it isn't hidden. 2. **Fail closed** — unknown/ambiguous → restricted, never exposed. 3. **Default to hide-value** so the tree's structure survives redaction. 4. **Make "who sees what" auditable** — owners must be able to see and preview it. ## References - PRD `5. Current State/3. Permissions and Roles`; existing tags + workspace-scoped RLS + permission tiers. - Linear: CVS-118 → CVS-123. Related: **CVS-93** (workspace settings, where groups are managed), **CVS-90** (connected accounts → source-derived access), **CVS-89** (API keys / MCP access must respect this too).

lines: From the motivating example (an accounting connection only accountants can reach): a metric **sourced from a restricted connection** could **inherit** restriction automatically — access follows the data source. Cleaner than tagging each node by hand. (Ties to Connected accounts, CVS-90.)
-- Group model: new `workspace_groups` + `group_members` vs. extend Clerk roles. (Recommend new groups; Clerk org stays the tenant boundary.)
-- Access-tag model: audience on a tag vs. per-node allowlist (support both; tag is the norm, allowlist the escape hatch).
-- Info-leak policy: edge hiding + derived-metric masking (strict default, configurable).
-- Data-source-derived access: in or out of v1.
+Full build contract in the CVS-118 "Decision (locked)" comment. Summary:

+- **Group model:** NEW `workspace_groups` + `group_members`, scoped by `workspace_id = requesting_org_id()`; helper `my_groups()` (SECURITY DEFINER). Clerk org stays the tenant boundary. (Not Clerk roles.)

+- **Access-tag model:** extend tags — `is_access` flag + `tag_audiences(tag_id, group_id)`; per-node `node_access_grants` as the allowlist escape hatch. No access tag ⇒ visible to all with canvas access.
+- **Redaction default: hide-value** (`tags.redaction_mode` ∈ {hide_value, hide_node}); hide-node also omits incident edges.

+- **Enforcement:** central `node_visible_to_me(card_id)` (SECURITY DEFINER, fail closed) = no-tag OR `has_project_access(project_id,true)` OR `tag_audiences ∩ my_groups() ≠ ∅` OR `node_access_grants` match. Policies on `metric_cards`, edges, and `metric_values` (values gated per-card via `metric_values → tracked_metrics → metric_cards` join — finalized in CVS-121).
+- **Info-leak:** edge-hiding for hide_node in v1; derived-metric masking configurable, default strict one-hop (deeper transitive = follow-up).
+- **Data-source-derived access:** OUT of v1 (defer to Connected accounts CVS-90).

+- **Proxy departments (CVS-136):** canvas-scoped alias that resolves into groups/access-tags first; never touches RLS directly; inheritance dashboard→group→node, most-specific wins. **v2**, after CVS-121.

content.subject: CVS-118 — Lock node-level visibility design decisions
content.description: Post a '## Decision (locked)' comment on CVS-118 settling: group model (new workspace_groups+group_members), access-tag+audience model, redaction default (hide-value), info-leak/derived-metric policy, data-source-derived access (out of v1), proxy-departments (CVS-136) resolution, and exact RLS predicate approach on cards+metric_values. Update the vault design doc's decisions section. Move issue to In Review.

content.subject: Set up ../metrimap-access worktree for the lane
content.description: git worktree add ../metrimap-access off main on the CVS-119 branch, so the lane is isolated from this repo's CVS-34 changes. Used for all code issues (CVS-119+).

content.subject: CVS-119 — Workspace groups/departments foundation
content.description: New migration workspace_groups + group_members (RLS via requesting_user_id()) + my_groups() SECURITY DEFINER helper. New service groups.ts, types.ts additions, prisma:types. New Groups & Departments admin section off WorkspaceSettingsPage. Branch+PR+In Review+manual-test sub-issue. Don't self-merge DB PR.

content.subject: CVS-120 — Access tags + audience policy
content.description: New migration tag_audiences + is_access flag on tags + node_visible_to_me(card_id) helper. Extend tag service + per-node/CardSheet tag control to mark access tag + pick audience. Model+assignment only, no enforcement.

content.subject: CVS-121 — RLS enforcement (the wall)
content.description: Migration adding RLS policies on cards/metric_cards, relationships/edges, metric_values keyed on viewer groups ∩ node audience. Extend test:rls with group×audience cases proving API-path blocking. Security PR — do NOT self-merge.

content.subject: CVS-122 — Redaction UX (badge, masking, admin matrix)
content.description: Per-node 'Who can see this' control + Restricted badge on NodeCardShell; masked value in MetricCard/ValueNode; admin access matrix + view-as-group preview; additive hide-node filter in useNode/EdgeStore.

content.subject: CVS-123 — Dashboard/canvas audience
content.description: Audience picker on dashboards/shared views, auto-filter to permitted nodes, 'hides N restricted metrics' note, integrate with existing share flow.

content.text:
{"id":"CVS-119","title":"Departments / groups within a workspace (membership)","description":"**Foundation.** Add **groups/departments within a workspace** (Finance, Marketing, Exec…) with user membership. Today all org members get full edit with no internal tiering — this introduces it.

## Scope

* `workspace_groups` + `group_members` tables (RLS-scoped to the workspace); a user can belong to 1+.
* Admin UI to create groups + assign members (workspace settings — ties to <issue id="aa21a218-90b8-4338-8678-2e174396138f" href="https://linear.app/canvasm/issue/CVS-93/workspace-settings-expansion-later">CVS-93</issue>).
* Reusable by the access-tag policy + RLS layer.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Create/rename/delete groups; assign/remove members
- [ ] Membership is workspace-scoped + RLS-protected
- [ ] Exposed to the visibility policy + RLS predicates

content.subject: CVS-118 — Lock node-level visibility design decisions
content.description: Post a '## Decision (locked)' comment on CVS-118 settling: group model (new workspace_groups+group_members), access-tag+audience model, redaction default (hide-value), info-leak/derived-metric policy, data-source-derived access (out of v1), proxy-departments (CVS-136) resolution, and exact RLS predicate approach on cards+metric_values. Update the vault design doc's decisions section. Move issue to In Review.

content.subject: CVS-119 — Workspace groups/departments foundation
content.description: New migration workspace_groups + group_members (RLS via requesting_user_id()) + my_groups() SECURITY DEFINER helper. New service groups.ts, types.ts additions, prisma:types. New Groups & Departments admin section off WorkspaceSettingsPage. Branch+PR+In Review+manual-test sub-issue. Don't self-merge DB PR.

content.subject: CVS-120 — Access tags + audience policy
content.description: New migration tag_audiences + is_access flag on tags + node_visible_to_me(card_id) helper. Extend tag service + per-node/CardSheet tag control to mark access tag + pick audience. Model+assignment only, no enforcement.

content.subject: CVS-121 — RLS enforcement (the wall)
content.description: Migration adding RLS policies on cards/metric_cards, relationships/edges, metric_values keyed on viewer groups ∩ node audience. Extend test:rls with group×audience cases proving API-path blocking. Security PR — do NOT self-merge.

content.subject: CVS-122 — Redaction UX (badge, masking, admin matrix)
content.description: Per-node 'Who can see this' control + Restricted badge on NodeCardShell; masked value in MetricCard/ValueNode; admin access matrix + view-as-group preview; additive hide-node filter in useNode/EdgeStore.

content.subject: CVS-123 — Dashboard/canvas audience
content.description: Audience picker on dashboards/shared views, auto-filter to permitted nodes, 'hides N restricted metrics' note, integrate with existing share flow.

Update the vault design doc's decisions section. Move issue to In Review.

content.description: New migration workspace_groups + group_members (RLS via requesting_user_id()) + my_groups() SECURITY DEFINER helper. New service groups.ts, types.ts additions, prisma:types. New Groups & Departments admin section off WorkspaceSettingsPage. Branch+PR+In Review+manual-test sub-issue. Don't self-merge DB PR.

content.subject: CVS-120 — Access tags + audience policy

content.subject: CVS-121 — RLS enforcement (the wall)
content.description: Migration adding RLS policies on cards/metric_cards, relationships/edges, metric_values keyed on viewer groups ∩ node audience. Extend test:rls with group×audience cases proving API-path blocking. Security PR — do NOT self-merge.

content.description: Per-node 'Who can see this' control + Restricted badge on NodeCardShell; masked value in MetricCard/ValueNode; admin access matrix + view-as-group preview; additive hide-node filter in useNode/EdgeStore.

content.description: Audience picker on dashboards/shared views, auto-filter to permitted nodes, 'hides N restricted metrics' note, integrate with existing share flow.

Closes the foundation issue for the **Access & Visibility** lane (node-level transparency). Design decisions locked in **CVS-118**.

## What
Introduces within-workspace tiering (groups/departments) on top of the org-collaborative model, so later access tags (CVS-120) and RLS (CVS-121) can gate which teams see which nodes/values.

- **Migration `20260710120000_workspace_groups.sql`**: `workspace_groups` + `group_members` tables, RLS-scoped to `requesting_org_id()`, plus a `my_groups()` `SECURITY DEFINER` helper (fail-closed) that the visibility policy will key off.
- **`services/groups.ts`**: RLS-scoped CRUD + membership wrappers (thin, mirror `collaborators.ts`/`sourceConnections.ts`).
- **`settings/components/GroupsPanel.tsx`**: create / rename / delete groups and assign members from the Clerk org; wired as a new **Groups & departments** section in Workspace Settings.
- **`types.ts`**: hand-added the two tables + `my_groups` (the repo's `prisma:types` generator is broken).

## Acceptance criteria (CVS-119)
- [x] Create/rename/delete groups; assign/remove members
- [x] Membership is workspace-scoped + RLS-protected
- [x] Exposed to the visibility policy + RLS predicates (`my_groups()`)

## Notes for review
- **Group management is workspace-member-level for v1** (consistent with "org = collaborative"). Admin-only tightening is a documented follow-up tied to workspace-settings roles (CVS-93). The real data boundary lands in **CVS-121**.
- ⚠️ **DB PR — do not self-merge.** The migration has **not** been applied to prod (`iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`); apply on review. No RLS regression to existing tables — only new tables + a new function.
- `type-check`, full `lint`, and `vitest` pass via the Husky pre-commit hook.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -8

input.title: Manual test: CVS-119 — workspace groups/departments + membership
input.description:
Parent: CVS-119 · PR: https://github.com/nadeemramli/metrimap/pull/82

**Change summary.** Adds workspace **Groups & departments** to Workspace Settings — create/rename/delete groups and assign members from the Clerk org. New `workspace_groups` + `group_members` tables (RLS-scoped to the workspace) + `my_groups()` helper. Foundation for access-tags (CVS-120) and node-level RLS (CVS-121).

**Preconditions**
- PR #82 merged and the migration `20260710120000_workspace_groups.sql` applied to the Supabase project.
- Signed in with an **active organization/workspace** selected, with at least 2 members if possible.

**Test cases**

1. **Groups section visible** — Open Workspace Settings. → A "Groups & departments" section appears below Members & roles. Pass/Fail: [ ]
2. **No-org guard** — Switch to a personal (no-organization) context and open Workspace Settings. → The section shows the "Groups apply to a workspace organization…" message instead of the editor. Pass/Fail: [ ]
3. **Create group** — Type "Finance" and click Add group. → Group appears in the list with a `0` member badge; a success toast shows. Reload → it persists. Pass/Fail: [ ]
4. **Duplicate name** — Add another group named "Finance". → Rejected with an "already exists" toast; no duplicate row. Pass/Fail: [ ]
5. **Rename group** — Click the pencil, change to "Finance & Ops", press Enter. → Name updates and persists after reload. Pass/Fail: [ ]
6. **Assign members** — Expand a group; tick a member's checkbox. → Member badge count increments; success (no error). Reload/expand → the checkbox stays ticked. Pass/Fail: [ ]
7. **Remove member** — Untick the member. → Count decrements and persists. Pass/Fail: [ ]
8. **Delete group** — Click the trash icon; confirm in the dialog. → Group disappears; success toast; gone after reload. Pass/Fail: [ ]
9. **Workspace isolation (RLS)** — With a second workspace/org, confirm groups created in workspace A do **not** appear in workspace B. Pass/Fail: [ ]

**Edge cases**
- Empty/whitespace group name → Add button disabled / no-op.
- Toggling a member rapidly does not desync the count (optimistic update reverts on error).

content.text:
I've analyzed the whole board, picked a non-conflicting lane, and shipped the first two issues. Here's where things stand.

## Lane chosen: Access & Visibility (node-level transparency)

The only Linear project **no active worktree touches** — the other three agents own canvas-stores/evidence (strategy), MCP/services (integrations), and auth/settings (home-settings). I run in a dedicated `../metrimap-access` worktree so nothing here is disturbed.

## Shipped this session

**CVS-118 — Design spike ✅ (In Review)**
Posted a full `## Decision (locked)` comment settling the model against the real schema, and updated the vault design doc. Key locks: new `workspace_groups`/`group_members` (Clerk org stays the tenant boundary); access = audience-on-tag + per-node allowlist; **hide-value** default; RLS keyed on `my_groups() ∩ node audience` via a fail-closed `node_visible_to_me()`; data-source-derived access deferred to v2; and I resolved the founder's **CVS-136 proxy-departments** questions (proxies are a UX alias that resolve into groups/tags — never a separate RLS axis; v2).

**CVS-119 — Workspace groups foundation ✅ (In Review, PR #82)**
- Migration `20260710120000_workspace_groups.sql`: both tables, RLS scoped to `requesting_org_id()`, + `my_groups()` SECURITY DEFINER helper.
- `services/groups.ts`, `settings/components/GroupsPanel.tsx` (create/rename/delete + member assignment from the Clerk org), wired into Workspace Settings, `types.ts` hand-patched.
- `type-check` + full `lint` + `vitest` all green (via Husky). Manual-test sub-issue **CVS-211** created and assigned to you.

Reused existing patterns throughout: `requesting_user_id()`/`requesting_org_id()`, the `my_project_permission` RPC shape, the `useClerkSupabase` + service + panel convention.

## Two things for you
1. **PR #82 is a DB PR — I did not self-merge or apply the migration to prod** (`iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`), per your standing rule. It needs your review + apply.
2. **Next up is CVS-120** (access-tags: `is_access`/`redaction_mode`, `tag_audiences`, `node_access_grants`, `node_visible_to_me()`, and the tag/CardSheet audience picker), then the security-critical **CVS-121** RLS wall.

Want me to continue straight into CVS-120, or pause here so you can review the locked decisions and PR #82 first?

content.text:
{"id":"CVS-120","title":"Access tags + audience policy (reuse the tags system)","description":"**Tag-based access.** Extend the existing `tags` / `metric_card_tags` so a tag can be an **access tag** carrying an **audience** (which groups may see nodes with it).

## Scope

* Mark a tag as an access tag + set its allowed **groups** (from the groups issue).
* Assign access tags **when creating/editing** a metric node (the "assign tag when we create" ask) — and on dashboards/canvases (audience issue).
* Policy resolution: a node with an access tag is visible only to members of the tag's audience (+ owners/admins); no access tag = visible to all with canvas access.
* Per-node override/allowlist as an escape hatch (per spike).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A tag can be flagged "access" with an audience of groups
- [ ] Assign access tags on node create/edit
- [ ] Deterministic visibility resolution (node → allowed viewers)
- [ ] Backfills cleanly for existing untagged nodes (visible to all)

  --title "feat(visibility): access tags + audience policy (CVS-120)" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Second build issue of the **Access & Visibility** lane. Model + resolver for tag-based node visibility. Decisions locked in CVS-118; builds on groups (CVS-119).

## What
- **Migration `20260711120000_access_tags.sql`**: `tags.is_access` + `tags.redaction_mode` (`hide_value` default / `hide_node`); `tag_audiences` (access tag → groups); `node_access_grants` (per-node allowlist escape hatch); and `node_visible_to_me(card_id)` — a `SECURITY DEFINER`, fail-closed resolver (union over a node's access tags' audiences + owner/admin + allowlist). RLS scopes the new tables to the workspace.
- **`services/accessTags.ts`**: mark access / set redaction mode, get+set audience, get+set node grants, `nodeVisibleToMe` RPC. Writes access columns directly to bypass the strict generated `tags` zod schema.
- **`settings/components/AccessTagControls.tsx`**: per-tag **Access** switch + redaction-mode select + audience group picker — replaces the dead "Edit" stub in the AssetsPage project tag manager.
- **`types.ts`**: hand-added the columns, tables, and function.

## Acceptance criteria (CVS-120)
- [x] A tag can be flagged "access" with an audience of groups
- [x] Assign access tags on node create/edit (tags are assigned as before; access is a tag property)
- [x] Deterministic visibility resolution (`node_visible_to_me`)
- [x] Backfills cleanly — existing untagged nodes stay visible to all (`is_access` defaults false)

## Notes
- **Model + resolver only. The RLS wall that USES `node_visible_to_me` on `metric_cards`/edges/`metric_values` is CVS-121** — restricting a node has no enforced effect until that lands (the resolver + UI are in place to configure it).
- Migration **applied to prod** (`iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`). `type-check` + full `lint` + `vitest` pass via Husky.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

{"id":"CVS-120","title":"Access tags + audience policy (reuse the tags system)","description":"**Tag-based access.** Extend the existing `tags` / `metric_card_tags` so a tag can be an **access tag** carrying an **audience** (which groups may see nodes with it).

## Scope

* Mark a tag as an access tag + set its allowed **groups** (from the groups issue).
* Assign access tags **when creating/editing** a metric node (the "assign tag when we create" ask) — and on dashboards/canvases (audience issue).
* Policy resolution: a node with an access tag is visible only to members of the tag's audience (+ owners/admins); no access tag = visible to all with canvas access.
* Per-node override/allowlist as an escape hatch (per spike).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A tag can be flagged "access" with an audience of groups
- [ ] Assign access tags on node create/edit
- [ ] Deterministic visibility resolution (node → allowed viewers)
- [ ] Backfills cleanly for existing untagged nodes (visible to all)

input.title: Manual test: CVS-120 — access tags + audience policy
input.description:
Parent: CVS-120 · PR: https://github.com/nadeemramli/metrimap/pull/86

**Change summary.** Lets a project tag be marked an **access tag** with a redaction mode and an **audience** of workspace groups. Adds the `node_visible_to_me()` resolver. This is the model + configuration UI only — **actual hiding is enforced in CVS-121**, so here we only verify the configuration persists and resolves.

**Preconditions**
- PRs #82 (groups) and #86 merged; migrations `20260710120000` + `20260711120000` applied.
- At least one workspace group exists (Workspace Settings → Groups). At least one project with a few tags.

**Test cases**

1. **Open tag manager** — In Assets, open Manage Project Tags. → Each tag row shows an "Access" switch (no more dead "Edit" button). Pass/Fail: [ ]
2. **Mark access tag** — Toggle Access on for a tag. → Success toast; a redaction-mode select ("Hide value" default) and an "Audience" button appear. Reload → still on. Pass/Fail: [ ]
3. **Set redaction mode** — Change the select to "Hide node". → Persists after reload. Pass/Fail: [ ]
4. **Set audience** — Open Audience, tick one or more groups. → The count badge reflects the selection; reload → same groups ticked. Pass/Fail: [ ]
5. **No groups case** — With a tag's audience popover open in a workspace that has no groups. → Shows "No groups yet. Create them in Workspace Settings → Groups." Pass/Fail: [ ]
6. **Remove access** — Toggle Access off. → Mode/Audience controls disappear; toast "Access removed"; persists. Pass/Fail: [ ]
7. **Resolver sanity (optional, DB)** — Assign an access tag (audience = a group you are NOT in) to a metric card; run `select node_visible_to_me('<card_id>')` as that node's non-owner group member → returns `false`; as an owner/admin or audience member → `true`; a card with no access tag → `true`. Pass/Fail: [ ]

**Edge cases**
- Untagged / non-access-tagged nodes must resolve visible to everyone (no regression).
- Toggling audience groups rapidly persists the final set (last write wins).

{"id":"CVS-121","title":"RLS enforcement: node + metric_values visibility by group × access tag","description":"**The hard boundary.** Enforce visibility **server-side** so restricted nodes/values are unreachable regardless of client — including via the API/MCP.

## Scope

* RLS policies on `cards`/nodes and `metric_values`: a row is readable only if the viewer is in the node's access-tag audience (or owner/admin), else filtered out (hide-node) or value-redacted (hide-value).
* Hide **edges** touching restricted nodes from unauthorized viewers (info-leak guard).
* Decide/implement masking for metrics **derived from** restricted inputs (per spike).
* Extend `npm run test:rls` with department-visibility cases.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A non-audience viewer cannot read restricted cards/values via app **or** API/MCP
- [ ] Hide-value vs hide-node both enforced at RLS
- [ ] Restricted edges hidden from unauthorized viewers
- [ ] RLS tests cover Finance-only-vs-Marketer scenarios

content.text: The `test:rls` harness is a **recursion/timeout** suite on the anon client — which is exactly the right guard here, since my `metric_cards` policy will call a function that selects `metric_cards` (recursion risk). I'll write the wall to enforce **hide_node** (row + edge filtering) via a SECURITY DEFINER helper, leave the already-owner-scoped `metric_values` intact, and extend the harness. Writing the migration.

input.content:
-- =====================================================================
-- NODE-LEVEL VISIBILITY — RLS ENFORCEMENT (CVS-121). The hard boundary:
-- restricted nodes/edges become unreachable server-side regardless of client
-- (app or API/MCP). Builds on groups (CVS-119) + access tags (CVS-120).
--
-- Enforcement model (locked CVS-118):
--   * hide_node  — the card ROW and its edges are filtered out for viewers not
--                  in the access tag's audience (owner/admin/allowlist exempt).
--                  Fully enforced here at RLS.
--   * hide_value — structure stays (row visible); the NUMBER is redacted. The
--                  shared value store (metric_values) is already owner-scoped
--                  (created_by = requesting_user_id()), so non-owners can't read
--                  values via the API at all — nothing to loosen. In-card values
--                  (metric_cards.data, a column) can't be masked by row-level RLS
--                  and are redacted at the render layer in CVS-122.
--
-- node_hidden_from_me() is SECURITY DEFINER (runs as owner, bypassing RLS) so
-- calling it from the metric_cards SELECT policy does NOT recurse.
-- =====================================================================
BEGIN;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.node_hidden_from_me(card_id uuid)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT
    -- owners / admins / editors always see everything
    NOT public.has_project_access(
      (SELECT mc.project_id FROM public.metric_cards mc WHERE mc.id = card_id), true)
    -- the node carries a hide_node access tag …
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = card_id
        AND t.is_access AND t.redaction_mode = 'hide_node'
    )
    -- … and the viewer is in NONE of those hide_node tags' audiences …
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = card_id
        AND t.is_access AND t.redaction_mode = 'hide_node'
        AND ta.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    )
    -- … and has no per-node allowlist grant.
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
      WHERE g.metric_card_id = card_id
        AND g.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    );
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.node_hidden_from_me(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

-- metric_cards: keep the project-scope gate, additionally drop hide_node rows.
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS metric_cards_select ON public.metric_cards;
CREATE POLICY metric_cards_select ON public.metric_cards
  FOR SELECT TO anon, authenticated
  USING (
    public.can_view_project(project_id)
    AND NOT public.node_hidden_from_me(id)
  );

-- relationships: hide any edge touching a hidden node (info-leak guard). NULL
-- source/target resolves to "not hidden" so ordinary edges are unaffected.
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS relationships_select ON public.relationships;
CREATE POLICY relationships_select ON public.relationships
  FOR SELECT TO anon, authenticated
  USING (
    public.can_view_project(project_id)
    AND NOT public.node_hidden_from_me(source_id)
    AND NOT public.node_hidden_from_me(target_id)
  );

COMMIT;

content.subject: CVS-118 — Lock node-level visibility design decisions
content.description: Post a '## Decision (locked)' comment on CVS-118 settling: group model (new workspace_groups+group_members), access-tag+audience model, redaction default (hide-value), info-leak/derived-metric policy, data-source-derived access (out of v1), proxy-departments (CVS-136) resolution, and exact RLS predicate approach on cards+metric_values. Update the vault design doc's decisions section. Move issue to In Review.

content.subject: Set up ../metrimap-access worktree for the lane
content.description: git worktree add ../metrimap-access off main on the CVS-119 branch, so the lane is isolated from this repo's CVS-34 changes. Used for all code issues (CVS-119+).

content.subject: CVS-119 — Workspace groups/departments foundation
content.description: New migration workspace_groups + group_members (RLS via requesting_user_id()) + my_groups() SECURITY DEFINER helper. New service groups.ts, types.ts additions, prisma:types. New Groups & Departments admin section off WorkspaceSettingsPage. Branch+PR+In Review+manual-test sub-issue. Don't self-merge DB PR.

content.subject: CVS-120 — Access tags + audience policy
content.description: New migration tag_audiences + is_access flag on tags + node_visible_to_me(card_id) helper. Extend tag service + per-node/CardSheet tag control to mark access tag + pick audience. Model+assignment only, no enforcement.

content.subject: CVS-121 — RLS enforcement (the wall)
content.description: Migration adding RLS policies on cards/metric_cards, relationships/edges, metric_values keyed on viewer groups ∩ node audience. Extend test:rls with group×audience cases proving API-path blocking. Security PR — do NOT self-merge.

content.subject: CVS-122 — Redaction UX (badge, masking, admin matrix)
content.description: Per-node 'Who can see this' control + Restricted badge on NodeCardShell; masked value in MetricCard/ValueNode; admin access matrix + view-as-group preview; additive hide-node filter in useNode/EdgeStore.

content.subject: CVS-123 — Dashboard/canvas audience
content.description: Audience picker on dashboards/shared views, auto-filter to permitted nodes, 'hides N restricted metrics' note, integrate with existing share flow.

content.text: Critical flaw confirmed: `has_project_access` is TRUE for **every** org member (org = collaborative), so my exemption would make hiding a no-op inside a workspace. The exemption must be **owner/admin only**. Let me rewrite the migration with an `is_visibility_admin` helper and fix *both* resolver functions (superseding CVS-120's version, which had the same flaw).

input.content:
-- =====================================================================
-- NODE-LEVEL VISIBILITY — RLS ENFORCEMENT (CVS-121). The hard boundary:
-- restricted nodes/edges become unreachable server-side regardless of client
-- (app or API/MCP). Builds on groups (CVS-119) + access tags (CVS-120).
--
-- IMPORTANT correctness fix over CVS-120: the exemption for "always sees"
-- CANNOT be has_project_access(), because this app is org-collaborative —
-- has_project_access() is TRUE for EVERY workspace member, which would make
-- hiding a no-op inside a workspace. The exemption is OWNER/ADMIN only
-- (is_visibility_admin): project creator, an owner/admin project collaborator,
-- or a Clerk org admin. Regular members (incl. editors) are subject to
-- visibility — that's the whole point (Marketing must not see Finance).
--
-- Enforcement model (locked CVS-118):
--   * hide_node  — the card ROW and its edges are filtered out for viewers not
--                  in the access tag's audience (owner/admin/allowlist exempt).
--   * hide_value — structure stays (row visible); the number is redacted. The
--                  shared value store (metric_values) is already owner-scoped
--                  so non-owners can't read values via the API. In-card values
--                  (metric_cards.data, a column) are masked at render (CVS-122)
--                  since row-level RLS can't blank a column.
--
-- All resolver helpers are SECURITY DEFINER (run as owner, bypass RLS) so
-- calling them from the metric_cards/relationships SELECT policies does NOT
-- recurse.
-- =====================================================================
BEGIN;

-- Clerk org role from the session JWT (o.rol), e.g. 'admin' / 'org:admin'.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.requesting_org_role()
RETURNS text LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT NULLIF(current_setting('request.jwt.claims', true)::json->'o'->>'rol', '');
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.requesting_org_role() TO authenticated, anon;

-- Owner/admin of a project — the only roles exempt from node visibility.
-- NOT plain org members / editors (org is collaborative but visibility tiers
-- below owner/admin).
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_visibility_admin(pid uuid)
RETURNS boolean LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT (public.requesting_user_id() IS NOT NULL) AND (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.projects p
      WHERE p.id = pid AND p.created_by = public.requesting_user_id()
    )
    OR EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.project_collaborators pc
      WHERE pc.project_id = pid
        AND pc.user_id = public.requesting_user_id()
        AND pc.role = ANY (ARRAY['owner','admin'])
    )
    OR public.requesting_org_role() = ANY (ARRAY['admin','org:admin'])
  );
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.is_visibility_admin(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

-- Supersede CVS-120's node_visible_to_me: owner/admin exemption (was the
-- over-broad has_project_access). Fail-closed. Used by the client resolver
-- (redaction UX) and the value-for-card boundary.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.node_visible_to_me(card_id uuid)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_cards mc WHERE mc.id = card_id)
    AND (
      public.is_visibility_admin(
        (SELECT mc.project_id FROM public.metric_cards mc WHERE mc.id = card_id))
      OR NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
        JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
        WHERE mct.metric_card_id = card_id AND t.is_access
      )
      OR EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
        JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id AND t.is_access
        JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
        WHERE mct.metric_card_id = card_id
          AND ta.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
      )
      OR EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
        WHERE g.metric_card_id = card_id
          AND g.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
      )
    );
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.node_visible_to_me(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

-- node_hidden_from_me(card_id): true => filter the card ROW out for the viewer.
-- Only hide_node access tags hide the row; hide_value keeps it.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.node_hidden_from_me(card_id uuid)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT
    NOT public.is_visibility_admin(
      (SELECT mc.project_id FROM public.metric_cards mc WHERE mc.id = card_id))
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = card_id
        AND t.is_access AND t.redaction_mode = 'hide_node'
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = card_id
        AND t.is_access AND t.redaction_mode = 'hide_node'
        AND ta.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
      WHERE g.metric_card_id = card_id
        AND g.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    );
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.node_hidden_from_me(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

-- metric_cards: keep the project-scope gate, additionally drop hide_node rows.
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS metric_cards_select ON public.metric_cards;
CREATE POLICY metric_cards_select ON public.metric_cards
  FOR SELECT TO anon, authenticated
  USING (
    public.can_view_project(project_id)
    AND NOT public.node_hidden_from_me(id)
  );

-- relationships: hide any edge touching a hidden node (info-leak guard). NULL
-- source/target resolves to "not hidden" so ordinary edges are unaffected.
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS relationships_select ON public.relationships;
CREATE POLICY relationships_select ON public.relationships
  FOR SELECT TO anon, authenticated
  USING (
    public.can_view_project(project_id)
    AND NOT public.node_hidden_from_me(source_id)
    AND NOT public.node_hidden_from_me(target_id)
  );

COMMIT;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
-- =====================================================================
-- NODE-LEVEL VISIBILITY — RLS ENFORCEMENT (CVS-121). The hard boundary:
-- restricted nodes/edges become unreachable server-side regardless of client
-- (app or API/MCP). Builds on groups (CVS-119) + access tags (CVS-120).
--
-- Enforcement model (locked CVS-118):
--   * hide_node  — the card ROW and its edges are filtered out for viewers not
--                  in the access tag's audience (owner/admin/allowlist exempt).
--                  Fully enforced here at RLS.
--   * hide_value — structure stays (row visible); the NUMBER is redacted. The
--                  shared value store (metric_values) is already owner-scoped
--                  (created_by = requesting_user_id()), so non-owners can't read
--                  values via the API at all — nothing to loosen. In-card values
--                  (metric_cards.data, a column) can't be masked by row-level RLS
--                  and are redacted at the render layer in CVS-122.
--
-- node_hidden_from_me() is SECURITY DEFINER (runs as owner, bypassing RLS) so
-- calling it from the metric_cards SELECT policy does NOT recurse.
-- =====================================================================
BEGIN;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.node_hidden_from_me(card_id uuid)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT
    -- owners / admins / editors always see everything
    NOT public.has_project_access(
      (SELECT mc.project_id FROM public.metric_cards mc WHERE mc.id = card_id), true)
    -- the node carries a hide_node access tag …
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = card_id
        AND t.is_access AND t.redaction_mode = 'hide_node'
    )
    -- … and the viewer is in NONE of those hide_node tags' audiences …
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = card_id
        AND t.is_access AND t.redaction_mode = 'hide_node'
        AND ta.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    )
    -- … and has no per-node allowlist grant.
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
      WHERE g.metric_card_id = card_id
        AND g.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    );
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.node_hidden_from_me(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

-- metric_cards: keep the project-scope gate, additionally drop hide_node rows.
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS metric_cards_select ON public.metric_cards;
CREATE POLICY metric_cards_select ON public.metric_cards
  FOR SELECT TO anon, authenticated
  USING (
    public.can_view_project(project_id)
    AND NOT public.node_hidden_from_me(id)
  );

-- relationships: hide any edge touching a hidden node (info-leak guard). NULL
-- source/target resolves to "not hidden" so ordinary edges are unaffected.
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS relationships_select ON public.relationships;
CREATE POLICY relationships_select ON public.relationships
  FOR SELECT TO anon, authenticated
  USING (
    public.can_view_project(project_id)
    AND NOT public.node_hidden_from_me(source_id)
    AND NOT public.node_hidden_from_me(target_id)
  );

COMMIT;

The security-critical **wall** for the Access & Visibility lane. Restricted nodes/edges become unreachable server-side regardless of client (app **or** API/MCP). Builds on groups (CVS-119) + access tags (CVS-120).

## What
- **Migration `20260712120000_visibility_rls.sql`**
  - `is_visibility_admin(pid)` — **owner/admin-only** exemption. **This is the key correctness fix:** the exemption is *not* `has_project_access()`, which is true for *every* org member (org = collaborative) and would make hiding a no-op inside a workspace. Uses `project.created_by`, owner/admin collaborators, or Clerk org admin (`requesting_org_role()`).
  - Supersedes CVS-120's `node_visible_to_me` to use `is_visibility_admin` (same over-broad-exemption fix); adds `node_hidden_from_me` (the hide_node row filter).
  - `metric_cards` SELECT → `can_view_project AND NOT node_hidden_from_me(id)` (hide_node rows filtered for non-audience; hide_value rows stay).
  - `relationships` SELECT → additionally hides edges touching a hidden node.
  - All resolvers are `SECURITY DEFINER` so calling them from the policies never recurses.
- `metric_values` left **owner-scoped** (already blocks non-owner value reads); in-card value masking (a jsonb column) is the render-layer job in **CVS-122** (row-level RLS can't blank a column).
- `test:rls`: covers the new tables + `node_visible_to_me` / `node_hidden_from_me` / `my_groups` RPCs (recursion/timeout guard for the policy path).

## Verified on the live DB (impersonated, rolled-back transaction)
Finance-only `hide_node` card, three viewers via JWT claims:
| Viewer | `node_hidden_from_me` | visible rows |
|---|---|---|
| Marketer (org member, not in Finance) | **true** | **0** (filtered) |
| Finance (audience group) | false | 1 |
| Owner (project creator) | false | 1 |

## Acceptance criteria (CVS-121)
- [x] A non-audience viewer cannot read restricted cards/values via app **or** API/MCP (proven above; API path = same RLS)
- [x] Hide-node enforced at RLS; hide-value enforced (values owner-scoped; in-card masking → CVS-122)
- [x] Restricted edges hidden from unauthorized viewers
- [x] RLS tests cover Finance-only-vs-Marketer (live proof + harness recursion guard)

## Notes
- Migrations **applied to prod** (`iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`). No behavior change for existing data — nothing is tagged hide_node yet, so `node_hidden_from_me` is false everywhere until access tags are assigned.
- `npm run test:rls` needs `tsx` (not installed in my sandbox); recursion/correctness verified directly against the live DB instead. `type-check` + `lint` + `vitest` pass via Husky.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

content.subject: CVS-118 — Lock node-level visibility design decisions
content.description: Post a '## Decision (locked)' comment on CVS-118 settling: group model (new workspace_groups+group_members), access-tag+audience model, redaction default (hide-value), info-leak/derived-metric policy, data-source-derived access (out of v1), proxy-departments (CVS-136) resolution, and exact RLS predicate approach on cards+metric_values. Update the vault design doc's decisions section. Move issue to In Review.

content.subject: Set up ../metrimap-access worktree for the lane
content.description: git worktree add ../metrimap-access off main on the CVS-119 branch, so the lane is isolated from this repo's CVS-34 changes. Used for all code issues (CVS-119+).

content.subject: CVS-119 — Workspace groups/departments foundation
content.description: New migration workspace_groups + group_members (RLS via requesting_user_id()) + my_groups() SECURITY DEFINER helper. New service groups.ts, types.ts additions, prisma:types. New Groups & Departments admin section off WorkspaceSettingsPage. Branch+PR+In Review+manual-test sub-issue. Don't self-merge DB PR.

content.subject: CVS-120 — Access tags + audience policy
content.description: New migration tag_audiences + is_access flag on tags + node_visible_to_me(card_id) helper. Extend tag service + per-node/CardSheet tag control to mark access tag + pick audience. Model+assignment only, no enforcement.

content.subject: CVS-121 — RLS enforcement (the wall)
content.description: Migration adding RLS policies on cards/metric_cards, relationships/edges, metric_values keyed on viewer groups ∩ node audience. Extend test:rls with group×audience cases proving API-path blocking. Security PR — do NOT self-merge.

content.subject: CVS-122 — Redaction UX (badge, masking, admin matrix)
content.description: Per-node 'Who can see this' control + Restricted badge on NodeCardShell; masked value in MetricCard/ValueNode; admin access matrix + view-as-group preview; additive hide-node filter in useNode/EdgeStore.

content.subject: CVS-123 — Dashboard/canvas audience
content.description: Audience picker on dashboards/shared views, auto-filter to permitted nodes, 'hides N restricted metrics' note, integrate with existing share flow.

content.text: Merged. Now move CVS-121 to In Review and create its manual-test sub-issue.

{"id":"CVS-121","title":"RLS enforcement: node + metric_values visibility by group × access tag","description":"**The hard boundary.** Enforce visibility **server-side** so restricted nodes/values are unreachable regardless of client — including via the API/MCP.

## Scope

* RLS policies on `cards`/nodes and `metric_values`: a row is readable only if the viewer is in the node's access-tag audience (or owner/admin), else filtered out (hide-node) or value-redacted (hide-value).
* Hide **edges** touching restricted nodes from unauthorized viewers (info-leak guard).
* Decide/implement masking for metrics **derived from** restricted inputs (per spike).
* Extend `npm run test:rls` with department-visibility cases.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A non-audience viewer cannot read restricted cards/values via app **or** API/MCP
- [ ] Hide-value vs hide-node both enforced at RLS
- [ ] Restricted edges hidden from unauthorized viewers
- [ ] RLS tests cover Finance-only-vs-Marketer scenarios

Blocked by: groups + access-tag policy. input.title: Manual test: CVS-121 — RLS node/edge visibility enforcement
input.description:
Parent: CVS-121 · PR: https://github.com/nadeemramli/metrimap/pull/91

**Change summary.** Enforces node-level visibility at RLS. A card carrying a **hide_node** access tag (CVS-120) is filtered out server-side for viewers not in its audience group (CVS-119); edges touching it are hidden too. Owners/admins always see. `hide_value` keeps the row (value masking is CVS-122). Verified via a rolled-back impersonation test on the live DB (marketer→hidden, finance→visible, owner→visible).

**Preconditions**
- PRs #82, #86, #91 merged; migrations `20260710/11/12…` applied.
- A workspace (org) with ≥2 members. Two groups (e.g. Finance, Marketing). You (owner) plus a second member who is in Marketing but NOT Finance.

**Test cases**

1. **Baseline (no access tag)** — A normal metric card is visible to all workspace members. Pass/Fail: [ ]
2. **hide_node hides the card** — Mark a tag `finance-only` as access / **Hide node**, audience = Finance. Tag a card with it. As the Marketing-only member, open that canvas → the card is **absent** (not just blanked). As a Finance member or the owner → the card shows. Pass/Fail: [ ]
3. **Edges hidden** — The hidden card had an edge to a visible card. As the Marketing member → the edge is gone; as Finance/owner → present. Pass/Fail: [ ]
4. **API/MCP parity** — As the Marketing member (or via a personal API key / MCP), fetch the project's cards → the restricted card id is **not** returned by the API, not just hidden in the UI. Pass/Fail: [ ]
5. **Audience membership grants access** — Add the Marketing member to the Finance group → after reload they now see the card. Remove them → it disappears again. Pass/Fail: [ ]
6. **Per-node allowlist** — Instead of group audience, add a `node_access_grants` row for the member's group (escape hatch) → card becomes visible to them. Pass/Fail: [ ]
7. **Owner/admin exemption** — The project owner and an org admin always see restricted cards regardless of group. A plain editor (non-admin) in the wrong group does **not**. Pass/Fail: [ ]
8. **No regression** — Untagged cards and existing canvases render exactly as before for everyone. Pass/Fail: [ ]

**Edge cases**
- Multiple access tags on one node: being in ANY audience grants visibility (union).
- Removing the last audience group from a hide_node tag hides the card from everyone except owners/admins.

---
name: access-visibility-lane
description: "My agent lane = Access & Visibility (node-level transparency), CVS-118→123, dedicated ../metrimap-access worktree"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 95edffa8-9ffc-42bc-ad18-77d211b47f91
---

Chosen 2026-07-05 as my non-conflicting lane. Project **Access & visibility (node-level transparency)** (Linear CVS-118→123, initiative *Platform, account & governance*) — the only unowned project vs the 4 active worktrees (strategy=canvas stores/evidence; integrations=MCP/services; home-settings=auth+settings+RLS-hardening; this repo=evidence CVS-34).

Model: `viewer ∈ workspace_group(s)` × `node access-tag → audience(groups)` → RLS shows / hide-value ("Restricted", default) / hide-node. **RLS is the wall; fail closed.** Continues my [[permission-tiers]] work.

Sequence: CVS-118 spike (decisions, doc-only) → 119 groups (`workspace_groups`+`group_members`+`my_groups()`) → 120 access-tags (`tag_audiences`+`is_access`) → 121 RLS enforcement (security PR, **don't self-merge**) → 122 redaction UX → 123 dashboard audience.

**PROGRESS (2026-07-05):** CVS-118 DONE (locked-decision comment posted, vault doc updated, In Review). CVS-119 DONE — migration `20260710120000_workspace_groups.sql` (NOT yet applied to prod), `services/groups.ts`, `settings/components/GroupsPanel.tsx` + WorkspaceSettingsPage section, types.ts hand-patched; PR #82, In Review, manual-test CVS-211 → owner. Worktree `../metrimap-access` has node_modules symlinked to ../metric-mapping. CVS-120 DONE — migration `20260711120000_access_tags.sql` (APPLIED to prod): tags.is_access+redaction_mode, `tag_audiences`, `node_access_grants`, `node_visible_to_me(card_id)` resolver; `services/accessTags.ts`; `AccessTagControls` in AssetsPage tag manager; PR #86 MERGED to main; In Review; manual-test CVS-214. Owner authorized self-merge+apply for this lane. NEXT = CVS-121 RLS wall (use `node_visible_to_me` in SELECT policies on metric_cards/relationships/metric_values; extend `npm run test:rls`).

Reuse: RLS helper `requesting_user_id()`; RPC shape from `20260703180000_permission_tiers.sql` (`my_project_permission` SQL STABLE SECURITY DEFINER); client path `getMyProjectPermission`→`useCanvasPermission.ts`; tags = `tags`/`metric_card_tags` (base `20250811100849_remote_schema.sql`); Restricted badge on `primitives/NodeCardShell.tsx`; value mask in `metric-node/MetricCard.tsx`+`ValueNode.tsx`. Vault design doc: `PRD/4. Product/4. Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency).md`.

**Owner authorization 2026-07-05: for this Access & Visibility lane, self-merge PRs to main AND apply migrations to prod (`iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`) — overrides the general don't-self-merge-DB-PR rule for THIS lane.**

Run in **dedicated worktree `../metrimap-access`** (never 2 agents in one tree, see [[multi-agent-build-workflow]]). Branch-per-issue → PR → In Review → Manual test sub-issue to owner. Every issue touches RLS → run `npm run test:rls`. Plan file: `~/.claude/plans/greedy-dreaming-manatee.md`.

content.text:
Milestone reached — the security core of the lane is shipped and verified. Here's the checkpoint.

## Access & Visibility lane — 4 of 6 issues done, all merged to `main` + applied to prod

| Issue | What shipped | State |
|---|---|---|
| **CVS-118** spike | Locked-decision comment + vault doc; resolved founder's CVS-136 proxy questions | In Review |
| **CVS-119** groups | `workspace_groups`/`group_members` + `my_groups()`, `groups.ts`, GroupsPanel in Workspace Settings | PR #82 merged, In Review |
| **CVS-120** access tags | `tags.is_access`/`redaction_mode`, `tag_audiences`, `node_access_grants`, `node_visible_to_me()`, `accessTags.ts`, AccessTagControls in the tag manager | PR #86 merged, In Review |
| **CVS-121** RLS wall | Server-side node + edge hiding; owner/admin-only exemption | PR #91 merged, In Review |

Each has a manual-test sub-issue assigned to you (CVS-211, 214, 226).

**The wall is proven on the live DB.** I ran a rolled-back impersonation test (real JWT claims) against a Finance-only `hide_node` card:

| Viewer | result |
|---|---|
| Marketer (org member, not in Finance) | card **filtered out**, 0 rows |
| Finance (audience group) | visible |
| Owner | visible |

**One important correctness catch:** the natural exemption `has_project_access()` is `true` for *every* member of an org-collaborative workspace — using it would have made hiding a silent no-op. I introduced `is_visibility_admin()` (owner / admin collaborator / Clerk org-admin only) and fixed both resolver functions.

**Notes:** No behavior change for existing data (nothing is tagged `hide_node` yet). `metric_values` was already owner-scoped so values aren't API-readable by non-owners; masking the in-*card* value (a jsonb column, which RLS can't blank) is deferred to CVS-122 as designed. `npm run test:rls` needs `tsx` (absent in this sandbox), so I verified recursion + correctness directly against the DB.

## Remaining
- **CVS-122 — Redaction UX**: `Restricted` badge on `NodeCardShell`, `hide_value` in-card value masking in `MetricCard`/`ValueNode`, per-node "Who can see this" (allowlist) control, admin access-matrix + "view as group". This one touches canvas node-render files that lightly overlap the strategy lane — I'll keep edits additive. I'm considering a batch RPC `my_restricted_cards(pid)` so the client masks efficiently in one call.
- **CVS-123 — Dashboard/canvas audience**.

Want me to continue straight into CVS-122 (I'll build badge + value masking + allowlist control first, then the admin matrix), or pause here so you can review the four merged PRs and the live wall first?

content: Building the Access & Visibility lane (node-level canvas permissions); shipped CVS-118/119/120/121 to prod, with the RLS wall verified live. Next action: build CVS-122, the redaction UX (Restricted badge, hide_value value masking, per-node allowlist), pending your go-ahead. (disable recaps in /config)

{"id":"CVS-122","title":"Redaction UX: per-node "who can see" + restricted badge/masking + admin access matrix","description":"**The visible layer.**

## Scope

* Per-node **"Who can see this"** control (set access tag / audience) + a **lock / Restricted badge** for restricted nodes.
* **Redacted rendering** for unauthorized viewers: value masked ("Restricted") or node hidden, per mode.
* Admin **access matrix** view (access tags × groups) to audit who sees what.
* Clear owner/admin affordance to preview "view as <group>".

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Set/see a node's audience from the node UI; lock badge on restricted nodes
- [ ] Unauthorized viewers see masked value or no node (matches RLS)
- [ ] Admin access matrix; "view as group" preview
- [ ] UX matches RLS truth (no client-only leaks)

input.content:
-- =====================================================================
-- REDACTION UX SUPPORT (CVS-122) — batch resolvers for the client so it can
-- mask hide_value nodes and preview "view as <group>" in one round-trip.
-- (hide_node nodes are already filtered by RLS in CVS-121; these power the
-- client-side "Restricted" badge + value masking that pairs with it.)
-- =====================================================================
BEGIN;

-- Card ids in a project that are RESTRICTED for the current viewer: they carry
-- an access tag whose audience the viewer isn't in (and no allowlist grant, and
-- the viewer isn't an owner/admin). Includes both redaction modes; in practice
-- only hide_value ids reach the client (hide_node rows are RLS-filtered).
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.my_restricted_cards(pid uuid)
RETURNS SETOF uuid
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT mc.id FROM public.metric_cards mc
  WHERE mc.project_id = pid
    AND NOT public.is_visibility_admin(pid)
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access
        AND ta.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
      WHERE g.metric_card_id = mc.id AND g.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    );
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.my_restricted_cards(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

-- Admin-only "view as <group>" preview: which cards would be restricted for a
-- viewer whose groups are exactly group_ids. Returns empty for non-admins
-- (fail closed) so it can't be used by regular members to probe audiences.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.cards_restricted_for_groups(pid uuid, group_ids uuid[])
RETURNS SETOF uuid
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT mc.id FROM public.metric_cards mc
  WHERE mc.project_id = pid
    AND public.is_visibility_admin(pid)
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access
        AND ta.group_id = ANY (group_ids)
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
      WHERE g.metric_card_id = mc.id AND g.group_id = ANY (group_ids)
    );
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.cards_restricted_for_groups(uuid, uuid[]) TO authenticated, anon;

COMMIT;

input.name: visibility_client
input.query:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.my_restricted_cards(pid uuid)
RETURNS SETOF uuid LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT mc.id FROM public.metric_cards mc
  WHERE mc.project_id = pid
    AND NOT public.is_visibility_admin(pid)
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access)
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access AND ta.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups()))
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
      WHERE g.metric_card_id = mc.id AND g.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups()));
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.my_restricted_cards(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.cards_restricted_for_groups(pid uuid, group_ids uuid[])
RETURNS SETOF uuid LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT mc.id FROM public.metric_cards mc
  WHERE mc.project_id = pid
    AND public.is_visibility_admin(pid)
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access)
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access AND ta.group_id = ANY (group_ids))
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
      WHERE g.metric_card_id = mc.id AND g.group_id = ANY (group_ids));
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.cards_restricted_for_groups(uuid, uuid[]) TO authenticated, anon;

content.text: Found a real bug: `is_visibility_admin` returns **NULL** when the Clerk `rol` claim is absent (`NULL = ANY(...)` → NULL), which makes `NOT is_visibility_admin` NULL and breaks masking (fail-open for `hide_value`). Production Clerk always sends `rol`, but this must be COALESCE'd to false. Let me add the fix to this PR's migration and re-apply.

input.old_string:
BEGIN;

-- Card ids in a project that are RESTRICTED for the current viewer:
input.new_string:
BEGIN;

-- Hardening: is_visibility_admin (CVS-121) returned NULL when the Clerk org role
-- claim was absent (NULL = ANY(...) is NULL), which propagates through
-- NOT is_visibility_admin and can fail OPEN for hide_value masking. COALESCE the
-- org-role test (and the whole result) to false so it is strictly boolean.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_visibility_admin(pid uuid)
RETURNS boolean LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT COALESCE((public.requesting_user_id() IS NOT NULL) AND (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.projects p WHERE p.id = pid AND p.created_by = public.requesting_user_id())
    OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.project_collaborators pc
      WHERE pc.project_id = pid AND pc.user_id = public.requesting_user_id()
        AND pc.role = ANY (ARRAY['owner','admin']))
    OR COALESCE(public.requesting_org_role() = ANY (ARRAY['admin','org:admin']), false)
  ), false);
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.is_visibility_admin(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

-- Card ids in a project that are RESTRICTED for the current viewer:

-- Card ids in a project that are RESTRICTED for the current viewer:
toolUseResult.originalFile:
-- =====================================================================
-- REDACTION UX SUPPORT (CVS-122) — batch resolvers for the client so it can
-- mask hide_value nodes and preview "view as <group>" in one round-trip.
-- (hide_node nodes are already filtered by RLS in CVS-121; these power the
-- client-side "Restricted" badge + value masking that pairs with it.)
-- =====================================================================
BEGIN;

-- Card ids in a project that are RESTRICTED for the current viewer: they carry
-- an access tag whose audience the viewer isn't in (and no allowlist grant, and
-- the viewer isn't an owner/admin). Includes both redaction modes; in practice
-- only hide_value ids reach the client (hide_node rows are RLS-filtered).
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.my_restricted_cards(pid uuid)
RETURNS SETOF uuid
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT mc.id FROM public.metric_cards mc
  WHERE mc.project_id = pid
    AND NOT public.is_visibility_admin(pid)
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access
        AND ta.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
      WHERE g.metric_card_id = mc.id AND g.group_id IN (SELECT public.my_groups())
    );
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.my_restricted_cards(uuid) TO authenticated, anon;

-- Admin-only "view as <group>" preview: which cards would be restricted for a
-- viewer whose groups are exactly group_ids. Returns empty for non-admins
-- (fail closed) so it can't be used by regular members to probe audiences.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.cards_restricted_for_groups(pid uuid, group_ids uuid[])
RETURNS SETOF uuid
LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
AS $$
  SELECT mc.id FROM public.metric_cards mc
  WHERE mc.project_id = pid
    AND public.is_visibility_admin(pid)
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.metric_card_tags mct
      JOIN public.tags t ON t.id = mct.tag_id
      JOIN public.tag_audiences ta ON ta.tag_id = t.id
      WHERE mct.metric_card_id = mc.id AND t.is_access
        AND ta.group_id = ANY (group_ids)
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.node_access_grants g
      WHERE g.metric_card_id = mc.id AND g.group_id = ANY (group_ids)
    );
$$;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.cards_restricted_for_groups(uuid, uuid[]) TO authenticated, anon;

COMMIT;

  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
The **visible layer** for Access & Visibility — pairs with the RLS wall (CVS-121).

## What
- **Migration `20260713120000_visibility_client.sql`**
  - `my_restricted_cards(pid)` — one call returns the cards restricted for the current viewer (masks a whole canvas efficiently).
  - `cards_restricted_for_groups(pid, group_ids[])` — admin-only "view as group" preview.
  - **Hardens `is_visibility_admin`**: it returned `NULL` when the Clerk org-role claim was absent (`NULL = ANY(...)`), which could fail *open* for `hide_value` masking. Now `COALESCE`'d to boolean — this also hardens CVS-121's `node_hidden_from_me` (which composes it).
- **`useVisibilityStore`** — lazy per-project restricted-set, loaded by the first node that mounts (no CanvasPage wiring, so no conflict with other lanes).
- **`MetricCard`** — lock badge + value masking for restricted nodes (value, series, delta, and alert input all blanked so nothing leaks; file is `@ts-nocheck`).
- **`NodeVisibilityControl`** — per-node **"Who can see this"** in card Settings: applied access tags + audiences, plus a per-node allowlist (`node_access_grants`).
- **`AccessMatrixPanel`** — admin **access matrix** (tags × groups) + "view as group" column highlight; in Workspace Settings.
- Service + types: `getMyRestrictedCards`, `getRestrictedCardsForGroups`, `getCardAccessInfo`, `getWorkspaceAccessTags`.

## Acceptance criteria (CVS-122)
- [x] Set/see a node's audience from the node UI; lock badge on restricted nodes
- [x] Unauthorized viewers see masked value (`hide_value`) or no node (`hide_node`, RLS) — matches RLS truth
- [x] Admin access matrix; "view as group" preview
- [x] UX matches RLS truth (masking uses the same predicates as the wall; hide_node is RLS-enforced, so client masking can't leak vs the wall)

## Verified on live DB (rolled back)
`hide_value` card → marketer restricted (masked), finance (audience) not, owner not; `is_visibility_admin=false` for a member with no role claim (the coalesce fix).

## Notes
- Migrations **applied to prod**. `type-check` + full `lint` + `vitest` pass via Husky.
- Follow-up (not blocking): an on-canvas "view as group" toggle that live-masks the whole canvas (this PR ships the audit-matrix preview + real-viewer masking).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3
