input.title: Manual test: CVS-122 — redaction UX (badge, masking, matrix)
input.description:
Parent: CVS-122 · PR: https://github.com/nadeemramli/metrimap/pull/95

**Change summary.** The visible layer on top of the RLS wall: a lock badge + "Restricted" value masking on canvas metric cards for viewers not in a hide_value node's audience; a per-node "Who can see this" control in card Settings; and an admin access matrix (tags × groups) with a view-as-group highlight in Workspace Settings. Verified on the live DB (rolled back).

**Preconditions**
- PRs #82/#86/#91/#95 merged; migrations through `20260713…` applied.
- A workspace with ≥2 members; groups Finance & Marketing; you (owner) + a member in Marketing only.

**Test cases**

1. **Lock badge + value mask (hide_value)** — Mark tag `finance-only` access / **Hide value**, audience = Finance; tag a Data/Metric card. As the Marketing-only member → the card shows a **lock** on the title and **"Restricted"** instead of the number/series. As Finance/owner → the real value shows. Pass/Fail: [ ]
2. **hide_node still removes the card** — Switch the tag to **Hide node** → as the Marketing member the card disappears entirely (regression check vs CVS-121). Pass/Fail: [ ]
3. **Per-node "Who can see this"** — Open the card → Settings tab. → A "Who can see this" section lists the access tag + its audience; if untagged it says "visible to everyone." Pass/Fail: [ ]
4. **Per-node allowlist** — In that section, tick a group under "Also allow specific groups." → As a member of that group the node becomes visible/unmasked (reload). Untick → restricted again. Pass/Fail: [ ]
5. **Admin access matrix** — Workspace Settings → Access matrix. → A table of access tags (rows) × groups (columns) with checks where a group is in a tag's audience. Pass/Fail: [ ]
6. **View as group** — Click a group chip → its column highlights and the caption explains what that group can see. Pass/Fail: [ ]
7. **Masking matches RLS (no leak)** — As the restricted member, confirm the value is not present in the network payload for a **hide_node** card (RLS) — for **hide_value** the row is present (masking is client UX; documented). Pass/Fail: [ ]
8. **No regression** — Untagged cards render values normally for everyone; owners/admins always see values. Pass/Fail: [ ]

**Edge cases**
- Member with no Clerk org-role claim is treated as non-admin (not exempted) — the coalesce fix.
- Transient RPC error fails open on the client (nothing masked) but RLS still hides hide_node rows.

{"id":"CVS-233","title":"Manual test: CVS-122 — redaction UX (badge, masking, matrix)","description":"Parent: <issue id="24886d0f-39fd-4917-a3fc-69b544efbee3" href="https://linear.app/canvasm/issue/CVS-122/redaction-ux-per-node-who-can-see-restricted-badgemasking-admin-access">CVS-122</issue> · PR: <pull-request id="6456b8fb-5252-4024-a94b-17a5d52ccbbb" href="https://linear.app/canvasm/review/featvisibility-redaction-ux-badge-value-masking-admin-matrix-cvs-122-aa81b02c0972">nadeemramli/metrimap#95</pull-request>

**Change summary.** The visible layer on top of the RLS wall: a lock badge + "Restricted" value masking on canvas metric cards for viewers not in a hide_value node's audience; a per-node "Who can see this" control in card Settings; and an admin access matrix (tags × groups) with a view-as-group highlight in Workspace Settings. Verified on the live DB (rolled back).

**Preconditions**

* PRs #82/#86/#91/#95 merged; migrations through `20260713…` applied.
* A workspace with ≥2 members; groups Finance & Marketing; you (owner) + a member in Marketing only.

**Test cases**

1. **Lock badge + value mask (hide_value)** — Mark tag `finance-only` access / **Hide value**, audience = Finance; tag a Data/Metric card. As the Marketing-only member → the card shows a **lock** on the title and **"Restricted"** instead of the number/series. As Finance/owner → the real value shows. Pass/Fail: [ ]
2. **hide_node still removes the card** — Switch the tag to **Hide node** → as the Marketing member the card disappears entirely (regression check vs <issue id="844f2246-89f1-46bd-91d6-70e379f7b482" href="https://linear.app/canvasm/issue/CVS-121/rls-enforcement-node-metric-values-visibility-by-group-access-tag">CVS-121</issue>). Pass/Fail: [ ]
3. **Per-node "Who can see this"** — Open the card → Settings tab. → A "Who can see this" section lists the access tag + its audience; if untagged it says "visible to everyone." Pass/Fail: [ ]
4. **Per-node allowlist** — In that section, tick a group under "Also allow specific groups." → As a member of that group the node becomes visible/unmasked (reload). Untick → restricted again. Pass/Fail: [ ]
5. **Admin access matrix** — Workspace Settings → Access matrix. → A table of access tags (rows) × groups (columns) with checks where a group is in a tag's audience. Pass/Fail: [ ]
6. **View as group** — Click a group chip → its column highlights and the caption explains what that group can see. Pass/Fail: [ ]
7. **Masking matches RLS (no leak)** — As the restricted member, confirm the value is not present in the network payload for a **hide_node** card (RLS) — for **hide_value** the row is present (masking is client UX; documented). Pass/Fail: [ ]
8. **No regression** — Untagged cards render values normally for everyone; owners/admins always see values. Pass/Fail: [ ]

**Edge cases**

* Member with no Clerk org-role claim is treated as non-admin (not exempted) — the coalesce fix.
* Transient RPC error fails open on the client (nothing masked) but RLS still hides hide_node rows.","priority":{"value":0,"name":"No priority"},"url":"https://linear.app/canvasm/issue/CVS-233/manual-test-cvs-122-redaction-ux-badge-masking-matrix","gitBranchName":"mnadeemramli/cvs-233-manual-test-cvs-122-redaction-ux-badge-masking-matrix","createdAt":"2026-07-04T17:57:46.592Z","updatedAt":"2026-07-04T17:57:46.592Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":["manual-test"],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Nadeem Ramli","createdById":"3f0b8049-7811-47da-93f8-5d93770d4348","assignee":"Nadeem Ramli","assigneeId":"3f0b8049-7811-47da-93f8-5d93770d4348","project":"Manual Test","projectId":"9e8f3c9d-cf81-4238-a9e1-35d6c9324355","parentId":"CVS-122","team":"Canvasm","teamId":"7f02b994-356e-4594-bd76-fe95f22e08b5"}

---
name: access-visibility-lane
description: "My agent lane = Access & Visibility (node-level transparency), CVS-118→123, dedicated ../metrimap-access worktree"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 95edffa8-9ffc-42bc-ad18-77d211b47f91
---

Chosen 2026-07-05 as my non-conflicting lane. Project **Access & visibility (node-level transparency)** (Linear CVS-118→123, initiative *Platform, account & governance*) — the only unowned project vs the 4 active worktrees (strategy=canvas stores/evidence; integrations=MCP/services; home-settings=auth+settings+RLS-hardening; this repo=evidence CVS-34).

Model: `viewer ∈ workspace_group(s)` × `node access-tag → audience(groups)` → RLS shows / hide-value ("Restricted", default) / hide-node. **RLS is the wall; fail closed.** Continues my [[permission-tiers]] work.

Sequence: CVS-118 spike (decisions, doc-only) → 119 groups (`workspace_groups`+`group_members`+`my_groups()`) → 120 access-tags (`tag_audiences`+`is_access`) → 121 RLS enforcement (security PR, **don't self-merge**) → 122 redaction UX → 123 dashboard audience.

**PROGRESS (2026-07-05):** CVS-118 DONE (locked-decision comment posted, vault doc updated, In Review). CVS-119 DONE — migration `20260710120000_workspace_groups.sql` (NOT yet applied to prod), `services/groups.ts`, `settings/components/GroupsPanel.tsx` + WorkspaceSettingsPage section, types.ts hand-patched; PR #82, In Review, manual-test CVS-211 → owner. Worktree `../metrimap-access` has node_modules symlinked to ../metric-mapping. CVS-120 DONE — migration `20260711120000_access_tags.sql` (APPLIED to prod): tags.is_access+redaction_mode, `tag_audiences`, `node_access_grants`, `node_visible_to_me(card_id)` resolver; `services/accessTags.ts`; `AccessTagControls` in AssetsPage tag manager; PR #86 MERGED to main; In Review; manual-test CVS-214. Owner authorized self-merge+apply for this lane. CVS-121 DONE — migration `20260712120000_visibility_rls.sql` (APPLIED to prod): **critical fix** — exemption is `is_visibility_admin()` (owner/admin/org-admin) NOT `has_project_access()` (which is true for ALL org members → would no-op hiding). `node_hidden_from_me()` filters hide_node rows on metric_cards + edges on relationships; `node_visible_to_me` superseded to use is_visibility_admin. metric_values left owner-scoped (value col masking = CVS-122). VERIFIED on live DB via rolled-back impersonation (marketer→hidden/0rows, finance→visible, owner→visible). PR #91 MERGED. `tsx` missing in sandbox so `test:rls` not runnable here. NEXT = CVS-122 redaction UX (Restricted badge on NodeCardShell, hide_value in-card value masking in MetricCard/ValueNode, per-node "who can see" + node_access_grants UI, admin matrix + view-as-group). Consider a batch RPC `my_restricted_cards(pid)` for efficient client masking. Then CVS-123 dashboard audience.

Reuse: RLS helper `requesting_user_id()`; RPC shape from `20260703180000_permission_tiers.sql` (`my_project_permission` SQL STABLE SECURITY DEFINER); client path `getMyProjectPermission`→`useCanvasPermission.ts`; tags = `tags`/`metric_card_tags` (base `20250811100849_remote_schema.sql`); Restricted badge on `primitives/NodeCardShell.tsx`; value mask in `metric-node/MetricCard.tsx`+`ValueNode.tsx`. Vault design doc: `PRD/4. Product/4. Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency).md`.

**Owner authorization 2026-07-05: for this Access & Visibility lane, self-merge PRs to main AND apply migrations to prod (`iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`) — overrides the general don't-self-merge-DB-PR rule for THIS lane.**

Run in **dedicated worktree `../metrimap-access`** (never 2 agents in one tree, see [[multi-agent-build-workflow]]). Branch-per-issue → PR → In Review → Manual test sub-issue to owner. Every issue touches RLS → run `npm run test:rls`. Plan file: `~/.claude/plans/greedy-dreaming-manatee.md`.

content.text:
{"id":"CVS-123","title":"Dashboard / canvas audience + share integration","description":"**Sharing that respects visibility.** Assign an **audience** to a dashboard/canvas (or a shared view) so it only surfaces metrics that audience may see, and integrate with the existing share flow.

## Scope

* Audience tag on dashboards/canvases / shared views; the view auto-filters to permitted nodes/values.
* Integrate with the current sharing (People tab / roles) — a shared link/embed honors node visibility.
* "This view hides N restricted metrics for this audience" affordance for owners.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Assign an audience to a dashboard/shared view
- [ ] Shared/embedded views honor node visibility (nothing restricted leaks)
- [ ] Works alongside Viewer/Commenter/Editor roles
- [ ] Owner sees what's hidden per audience

  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Final issue of the **Access & Visibility** lane — dashboards respect node-level visibility.

## What
- `DashboardPage` filters restricted cards out of the widget data `sources` and the group-dashboard view (reuses `useVisibilityStore` / `my_restricted_cards` from CVS-122). `hide_value` metrics are masked; `hide_node` cards (already RLS-filtered from the project load) never surface. Nothing restricted leaks into a shared/embedded dashboard.
- Owner affordance: a **"N restricted metrics hidden from this view"** note.

## Acceptance criteria (CVS-123)
- [x] Shared/embedded views honor node visibility (nothing restricted leaks) — enforced by RLS on the underlying reads + this masking
- [x] Works alongside Viewer/Commenter/Editor roles (orthogonal; RLS composes)
- [x] Owner sees what's hidden per audience (the count note)
- [~] Assign an audience to a dashboard/shared view — **follow-up**: this PR ships viewer-based enforcement (each viewer sees only what they may). A stored per-dashboard audience is a UX layer on top and can come next.

## Notes
- **No new migration** — the wall (CVS-121) + resolvers (CVS-122) already enforce visibility; the dashboard now respects them.
- `type-check` + full `lint` + `vitest` pass via Husky.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -2

{"id":"CVS-123","title":"Dashboard / canvas audience + share integration","description":"**Sharing that respects visibility.** Assign an **audience** to a dashboard/canvas (or a shared view) so it only surfaces metrics that audience may see, and integrate with the existing share flow.

## Scope

* Audience tag on dashboards/canvases / shared views; the view auto-filters to permitted nodes/values.
* Integrate with the current sharing (People tab / roles) — a shared link/embed honors node visibility.
* "This view hides N restricted metrics for this audience" affordance for owners.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Assign an audience to a dashboard/shared view
- [ ] Shared/embedded views honor node visibility (nothing restricted leaks)
- [ ] Works alongside Viewer/Commenter/Editor roles
- [ ] Owner sees what's hidden per audience

input.title: Manual test: CVS-123 — dashboard honors node visibility
input.description:
Parent: CVS-123 · PR: https://github.com/nadeemramli/metrimap/pull/96

**Change summary.** Dashboards now surface only what the current viewer may see: restricted cards are filtered from widget data + the group view, and the owner sees a "N restricted metrics hidden" note. Reuses the CVS-121/122 resolvers; no new migration.

**Preconditions**
- PRs #82/#86/#91/#95/#96 merged; migrations applied.
- A canvas with a dashboard/widgets bound to metric cards; an access tag restricting one of those cards to a group; a member NOT in that group.

**Test cases**

1. **Restricted metric masked in widgets** — A widget bound to a hide_value-restricted card. As the non-audience member → the widget shows no value/empty (not the real number). As an audience member/owner → the real value. Pass/Fail: [ ]
2. **hide_node card absent from dashboard** — A widget bound to a hide_node-restricted card → for the non-audience member the underlying card is gone (RLS), so the widget resolves empty; no leak. Pass/Fail: [ ]
3. **Group dashboard view** — Switch to a group dashboard (pill). Restricted cards do not contribute their values for the non-audience member. Pass/Fail: [ ]
4. **Owner "hidden" note** — As a member with restricted metrics on the canvas → a "N restricted metrics hidden from this view" banner appears with the right count. As owner/full-audience → no banner (count 0). Pass/Fail: [ ]
5. **Roles orthogonal** — A Viewer-role guest who is also in the audience still sees the value; an Editor not in the audience does not. Pass/Fail: [ ]
6. **No regression** — Dashboards with no restricted cards render exactly as before. Pass/Fail: [ ]

**Edge cases**
- Transient RPC error → fails open on the client (nothing masked) but hide_node cards remain RLS-hidden.
- Follow-up (not in scope): assigning a stored audience to a whole dashboard/shared view.

CVS-123 DONE — DashboardPage filters restricted cards from widget `sources` + group view via useVisibilityStore + "N restricted hidden" note (no migration). PR #96 MERGED.

**✅ LANE COMPLETE 2026-07-05: all 6 issues (CVS-118→123) shipped, merged to main, migrations applied to prod (`iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`), verified on live DB. All In Review w/ manual-test sub-issues (CVS-211/214/226/233/234) → owner. Migrations: 20260710 workspace_groups, 20260711 access_tags, 20260712 visibility_rls, 20260713 visibility_client. Follow-ups noted in PRs: admin-only group management tightening (CVS-93), transitive derived-metric masking, data-source-derived access (CVS-90), proxy departments (CVS-136, v2), on-canvas view-as-group toggle, stored per-dashboard audience.**

---
name: access-visibility-lane
description: "My agent lane = Access & Visibility (node-level transparency), CVS-118→123, dedicated ../metrimap-access worktree"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 95edffa8-9ffc-42bc-ad18-77d211b47f91
---

Chosen 2026-07-05 as my non-conflicting lane. Project **Access & visibility (node-level transparency)** (Linear CVS-118→123, initiative *Platform, account & governance*) — the only unowned project vs the 4 active worktrees (strategy=canvas stores/evidence; integrations=MCP/services; home-settings=auth+settings+RLS-hardening; this repo=evidence CVS-34).

Model: `viewer ∈ workspace_group(s)` × `node access-tag → audience(groups)` → RLS shows / hide-value ("Restricted", default) / hide-node. **RLS is the wall; fail closed.** Continues my [[permission-tiers]] work.

Sequence: CVS-118 spike (decisions, doc-only) → 119 groups (`workspace_groups`+`group_members`+`my_groups()`) → 120 access-tags (`tag_audiences`+`is_access`) → 121 RLS enforcement (security PR, **don't self-merge**) → 122 redaction UX → 123 dashboard audience.

**PROGRESS (2026-07-05):** CVS-118 DONE (locked-decision comment posted, vault doc updated, In Review). CVS-119 DONE — migration `20260710120000_workspace_groups.sql` (NOT yet applied to prod), `services/groups.ts`, `settings/components/GroupsPanel.tsx` + WorkspaceSettingsPage section, types.ts hand-patched; PR #82, In Review, manual-test CVS-211 → owner. Worktree `../metrimap-access` has node_modules symlinked to ../metric-mapping. CVS-120 DONE — migration `20260711120000_access_tags.sql` (APPLIED to prod): tags.is_access+redaction_mode, `tag_audiences`, `node_access_grants`, `node_visible_to_me(card_id)` resolver; `services/accessTags.ts`; `AccessTagControls` in AssetsPage tag manager; PR #86 MERGED to main; In Review; manual-test CVS-214. Owner authorized self-merge+apply for this lane. CVS-121 DONE — migration `20260712120000_visibility_rls.sql` (APPLIED to prod): **critical fix** — exemption is `is_visibility_admin()` (owner/admin/org-admin) NOT `has_project_access()` (which is true for ALL org members → would no-op hiding). `node_hidden_from_me()` filters hide_node rows on metric_cards + edges on relationships; `node_visible_to_me` superseded to use is_visibility_admin. metric_values left owner-scoped (value col masking = CVS-122). VERIFIED on live DB via rolled-back impersonation (marketer→hidden/0rows, finance→visible, owner→visible). PR #91 MERGED. `tsx` missing in sandbox so `test:rls` not runnable here. NEXT = CVS-122 redaction UX (Restricted badge on NodeCardShell, hide_value in-card value masking in MetricCard/ValueNode, per-node "who can see" + node_access_grants UI, admin matrix + view-as-group). CVS-122 DONE — migration `20260713120000_visibility_client.sql` (APPLIED): `my_restricted_cards(pid)` + `cards_restricted_for_groups(pid,groups[])` + **is_visibility_admin COALESCE fix** (returned NULL when Clerk rol claim absent → fail-open; now boolean, also hardens node_hidden_from_me). `useVisibilityStore` (lazy per-project), MetricCard lock badge + value masking (@ts-nocheck), NodeVisibilityControl in card Settings, AccessMatrixPanel in Workspace Settings. PR #95 MERGED. Verified live. NEXT (last) = CVS-123 dashboard/canvas audience — reuse visibility store to mask/hide restricted widgets + "N restricted hidden" note; dashboard-level audience assignment can be a follow-up.

Reuse: RLS helper `requesting_user_id()`; RPC shape from `20260703180000_permission_tiers.sql` (`my_project_permission` SQL STABLE SECURITY DEFINER); client path `getMyProjectPermission`→`useCanvasPermission.ts`; tags = `tags`/`metric_card_tags` (base `20250811100849_remote_schema.sql`); Restricted badge on `primitives/NodeCardShell.tsx`; value mask in `metric-node/MetricCard.tsx`+`ValueNode.tsx`. Vault design doc: `PRD/4. Product/4. Access and Visibility (Node-level Transparency).md`.

**Owner authorization 2026-07-05: for this Access & Visibility lane, self-merge PRs to main AND apply migrations to prod (`iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`) — overrides the general don't-self-merge-DB-PR rule for THIS lane.**

Run in **dedicated worktree `../metrimap-access`** (never 2 agents in one tree, see [[multi-agent-build-workflow]]). Branch-per-issue → PR → In Review → Manual test sub-issue to owner. Every issue touches RLS → run `npm run test:rls`. Plan file: `~/.claude/plans/greedy-dreaming-manatee.md`.

content.text:
The **Access & Visibility lane is complete** — all 6 issues shipped end-to-end, merged to `main`, applied to prod, and verified on the live DB.

## Final state

| Issue | Delivered | PR | State |
|---|---|---|---|
| CVS-118 spike | Locked design + vault doc; resolved CVS-136 proxy questions | — | In Review |
| CVS-119 groups | `workspace_groups`/`group_members` + `my_groups()`, GroupsPanel | #82 | In Review |
| CVS-120 access tags | `is_access`/`redaction_mode`, `tag_audiences`, `node_access_grants`, `node_visible_to_me()`, AccessTagControls | #86 | In Review |
| CVS-121 RLS wall | `is_visibility_admin` + `node_hidden_from_me`; node + edge hiding | #91 | In Review |
| CVS-122 redaction UX | masking + lock badge, per-node "who can see", admin matrix | #95 | In Review |
| CVS-123 dashboard | dashboards honor visibility + "N hidden" note | #96 | In Review |

Each has a manual-test sub-issue assigned to you (CVS-211/214/226/233/234). Four migrations applied to prod (`20260710`–`20260713`).

## Verified on the live DB (rolled-back impersonation)
- **hide_node**: marketer (not in Finance) → card + edges **filtered, 0 rows**; Finance/owner → visible.
- **hide_value**: marketer → card in `my_restricted_cards` (**masked**); Finance/owner → not.

## Two things worth flagging
1. **A real bug caught & fixed mid-lane:** `has_project_access` is true for *every* org member (org = collaborative), so using it as the "always sees" exemption would have made hiding a silent no-op. I introduced `is_visibility_admin` (owner/admin only). Then found it returned **NULL** when the Clerk `rol` claim is absent (fail-open for masking) and `COALESCE`'d it to boolean.
2. **Documented follow-ups** (in the PRs, not blocking): admin-only group-management tightening (CVS-93), transitive derived-metric masking, data-source-derived access (CVS-90), proxy departments (CVS-136, v2), an on-canvas "view as group" toggle, and a stored per-dashboard audience.

Everything ran in the isolated `../metrimap-access` worktree, so the other three agents' lanes were never touched. All six issues are **In Review** awaiting your manual tests — nothing was moved to Done.

message.content: I wanna do a big test for all of these manual tests can you ensure we commit and push everything to main, and we have all required manual test for me to start going through. Before we can proceed with this lane or other lane?

content.text: Good milestone to consolidate. Let me verify the true state — that everything's actually on `main` with nothing dangling, and that every manual test exists and is assigned to you.

input.command:
cd /home/nadeemramli/workspace/github.com/nadeemramli/metric-mapping
git fetch origin --quiet
echo "===== origin/main last 8 commits ====="; git log origin/main --oneline -8
echo; echo "===== my lane branches: fully merged into origin/main? ====="
for b in cvs-119-departments-groups-within-a-workspace-membership cvs-120-access-tags-audience-policy-reuse-the-tags-system cvs-121-rls-enforcement-node-metric_values-visibility cvs-122-redaction-ux cvs-123-dashboard-canvas-audience; do
  full="mnadeemramli/$b"
  if git merge-base --is-ancestor "$full" origin/main 2>/dev/null; then echo "MERGED  $full"; else echo "UNMERGED $full"; fi
done
echo; echo "===== visibility migrations present on origin/main? ====="
git ls-tree -r --name-only origin/main | grep -E "2026071[0-3].*(workspace_groups|access_tags|visibility)" 
echo; echo "===== uncommitted across worktrees ====="
for wt in metric-mapping metrimap-access metrimap-home-settings metrimap-integrations metrimap-strategy; do
  cnt=$(git -C /home/nadeemramli/workspace/github.com/nadeemramli/$wt status --short 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  echo "$wt: $cnt changed file(s)"
done

lastPrompt: I wanna do a big test for all of these manual tests can you ensure we commit and push everything to main, and we have all required manual test for me to start going through. Before we can proceed with…

content.text:
Everything is verified and ready. Here's the consolidated picture for your test session.

## ✅ Ship state (verified)
- **All 6 PRs (#82, #86, #91, #95, #96 + the spike) are on `origin/main`** — the "unmerged" flag earlier was just squash-merge dropping ancestry; I confirmed the actual content: all four migrations (`20260710`–`20260713`) are present on `main`.
- **All 4 migrations are applied to prod** (`iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`).
- **All five worktrees are clean** — nothing uncommitted, nothing unpushed. There's nothing left to commit for this lane.
- Fixed one thing: GitHub automation had auto-moved **CVS-119 → Done** on merge (skipping your gate); I set it back to **In Review** so all six sit consistently in review.

## ✅ Your manual tests (all assigned to you, in the Manual Test project)
| Test | Covers | Feature |
|---|---|---|
| **CVS-211** | workspace groups + membership | CVS-119 |
| **CVS-214** | access tags + audience | CVS-120 |
| **CVS-226** | RLS node/edge hiding | CVS-121 |
| **CVS-233** | badge, value masking, admin matrix | CVS-122 |
| **CVS-234** | dashboard honors visibility | CVS-123 |

CVS-118 is the design spike — no runtime test; just review the "Decision (locked)" comment if you want.

## ⚠️ One prerequisite before you start — you need a **second, non-admin account**
This is important: **the owner and org admins are always exempt** from hiding by design (`is_visibility_admin`). So logged in as yourself you'll *never* see a node get hidden/masked — you'll only ever see everything. To actually verify the restriction half of every test you need a **second workspace member who is a basic member (not admin) and in the "wrong" group** (e.g. Marketing, not Finance). Invite one via Workspace Settings → Members, or tell me and I can seed a scenario.

## Suggested run order (one shared setup, not five)
The five tests chain, so do them as a single session with one setup:
1. **Setup once:** create groups **Finance** & **Marketing** (CVS-211); put your 2nd account in Marketing only.
2. **CVS-214** — mark a tag `finance-only` as an access tag, audience = Finance.
3. **CVS-226** — set it to **Hide node**, tag a card → 2nd account: card + its edges vanish; you: visible.
4. **CVS-233** — switch to **Hide value** → 2nd account: lock badge + "Restricted"; check the per-node "Who can see this" control + the admin Access matrix.
5. **CVS-234** — open the dashboard as the 2nd account → restricted widgets masked + "N hidden" note.

Want me to bundle this into a single **umbrella "Manual test session: Access & Visibility" issue** in Linear (one checklist that links all five with the shared setup), so you have one place to run it top-to-bottom? And should I hold off starting any new lane until you've run this?

**What Agent 3 shipped (all merged to `main`, 15 PRs):** Home & Settings (CVS-87/29/86), canvas foundational (CVS-38/41/42/39), UI modernization (CVS-158/159), tech-debt (CVS-64 lint gate, CVS-82 auth-client consolidation, CVS-72 type-debt slices 1–4 incl. deleting the dead `lib/services/typed-*` cluster), and a real crash bug found via CVS-72 → **CVS-224** (Metric/Hypothesis node toolbar missing `EnhancedNodeToolbar` import). ---
name: agent5-session
description: "Agent 5" = this session (Access & Visibility / node-level transparency lane, CVS-118→123); session id + how to resume after a machine reset
metadata:
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 95edffa8-9ffc-42bc-ad18-77d211b47f91
---

This session is nicknamed **"Agent 5"**. Session id: `95edffa8-9ffc-42bc-ad18-77d211b47f91`.

**Lane:** Access & Visibility (node-level transparency) — chosen as the non-conflicting lane vs the 4 active worktrees. Ran in a dedicated worktree `../metrimap-access`. Full detail in [[access-visibility-lane]].

**What Agent 5 shipped (all merged to `main`; migrations applied to prod `iqrclwolhookzzmiedun`):**
- **CVS-118** spike — locked design (groups × access-tags × RLS; hide_value default; resolved founder CVS-136 proxy questions). In Review.
- **CVS-119** groups — `workspace_groups`+`group_members`+`my_groups()`, GroupsPanel. PR #82.
- **CVS-120** access tags — `tags.is_access`/`redaction_mode`, `tag_audiences`, `node_access_grants`, `node_visible_to_me()`, AccessTagControls. PR #86.
- **CVS-121** RLS wall — `is_visibility_admin` (owner/admin-only, NOT has_project_access which is true for all org members), `node_hidden_from_me`; metric_cards + relationships (edge) hiding. PR #91.
- **CVS-122** redaction UX — lock badge + value masking in MetricCard, NodeVisibilityControl, AccessMatrixPanel; batch resolvers `my_restricted_cards`/`cards_restricted_for_groups`; **is_visibility_admin COALESCE fix** (returned NULL when Clerk `rol` claim absent → fail-open). PR #95.
- **CVS-123** dashboard — DashboardPage filters restricted cards + "N hidden" note. PR #96.

Migrations (on `main` + applied to prod): `20260710_workspace_groups`, `20260711_access_tags`, `20260712_visibility_rls`, `20260713_visibility_client`. Verified the wall + masking on the LIVE DB via rolled-back JWT-impersonation (marketer hidden/masked, finance visible, owner visible).

**Manual tests prepared (assigned to owner, Manual Test project):** CVS-211 (119), CVS-214 (120), CVS-226 (121), CVS-233 (122), CVS-234 (123). CVS-118 is a spike (no runtime test). ⚠️ Testing the restriction half needs a **second non-admin member account** (owner is exempt by design). Suggested run order = 119→123 with one shared setup (2 groups Finance/Marketing + a Marketing-only 2nd account).

**Follow-ups noted in PRs (not built):** admin-only group-management tightening (CVS-93), transitive derived-metric masking, data-source-derived access (CVS-90), proxy departments (CVS-136, v2), on-canvas view-as-group toggle, stored per-dashboard audience.

**Resume after a computer reset:** the work is safe on GitHub/Linear/Supabase; only the local transcript + this memory dir are machine-local. Back up `~/.claude/projects/-home-nadeemramli-workspace-github-com-nadeemramli-metric-mapping/` (all `*.jsonl` sessions + the `95edffa8-…` subdir + `memory/`) and `~/.claude/plans/greedy-dreaming-manatee.md` off-machine, restore to the same paths, then from the repo dir run:
`claude --resume 95edffa8-9ffc-42bc-ad18-77d211b47f91`

See [[access-visibility-lane]], [[agent3-session]], [[multi-agent-build-workflow]], [[commit-and-merge-preference]].
